SESSION COMPLETE

CHARISMA COMPANY: FIZZLEBLAST’S FIELDTRIP

A Charisma Company One-Shot

ONE-SHOT // THE CHULTAN EXPEDITION // ~4H
🛡️
Moor
Cleric • Kyle
📜
Aramil
Warlock • Kyle
🪓
Opal
Barbarian • Shannon
🔮
Lavender
Warlock • Kelsey
🗡️
Bob
Rogue • Josh
Session Recap

The Story So Far

The golden serpent hung from the archway like an offering to something older than prayer, its jeweled eyes catching the last light of a dying sun -- and one hand, quick and faithless, reached for what should never have been taken.

The canoes scraped against black sand, and the jungle screamed.

♦ ♦ ♦

The temple entrance gaped before them, flanked by heliographic thermal arrays — mirrors angled to focus sunlight into beams that could melt flesh from bone. Two gorallons, four-armed jungle apes with brows like cliff shelves, paced at the edge of the light, snarling. Their skin blistered where the beams caught them, but hunger kept them circling.

♦ ♦ ♦

The prism room was the temple's mechanical soul — a cathedral of redirected light and ancient engineering. A massive crystalline prism hung from a bar in the center of the vaulted space, splitting the beams that fed it into spectral lances. Two enormous stone snake statues flanked the room, their hollow eyes serving as reflectors. Four unlit gems crowned the far portcullis. The message was clear: light all four, and the way opens.

But the room had teeth.

♦ ♦ ♦

It emerged from the mouth of one of the stone serpent statues, pouring out like oil — a constrictor of impossible length, forty feet from blunt nose to tapered tail, its body thick as a man's torso. Heat had roused it from whatever ancient slumber the temple's climate had imposed. It tasted the air with a tongue like a black ribbon and turned its flat head toward the warmest bodies in the room.

♦ ♦ ♦

They climbed.

Through corridors thick with rising heat. Past the turning cranks that redirected the mirror beams. Past treasure hoards they did not have time to properly loot, though they grabbed what they could — a helm of telepathy for Aramil, an alchemy jug and a manual of quickness for Moor, a bloodaxe and periapt of wound closure for Opal, boots of striding and springing that would later save them all.

♦ ♦ ♦

The world turned orange. A roaring sphere of flame erupted from the ridgeline above — launched by one of the rival adventurers who had been watching, waiting, planning to let someone else do the hard work and steal the prize. The fireball missed Bob by inches, his reflexes bending his glide into a desperate roll. Half the damage. Survivable.

♦ ♦ ♦

Dawn should have come.

The party slept in shifts within the safety field, and when the hours passed and the sky remained the same flat, starless black it had been at midnight, no one said anything at first. Exhaustion has a way of excusing the impossible. Perhaps they had miscounted. Perhaps the jungle canopy was simply too thick.

But Aramil knew.

Session Flow

Timeline

Potion splash and Attunement

Energy Peaks

Table Energy Peaks

Top 10 Moments Ranked by Energy Score
1
10
The Escape by 4:20
The party completed the skill challenge escape sequence with exactly 4 minutes and 20 seconds to spare, barely surviving a gauntlet of traps, rival adventurers, and a rampaging T-Rex
Chaos Peak
2
9
Opal Crits the Snake Blind
Shannon's Opal had been blinded by a thermal mirror beam just minutes earlier — 14 radiant damage directly to the eyes. When the giant constrictor snake grappled Aramil and squeezed him to -10 HP, the situation was desperate.
Combat
3
8.5
Squirtons and Gushers
The very first energy spike came within the opening minutes, when the DM mentioned the party might be "hurting for a squirting" and Kelsey immediately said "that's what went in my head before you said it, 'squirtin' hahaha."
Comedy Peak
4
8.5
Bob Goes Batman
Bob grabbed the golden idol from above a sleeping T-Rex, jumped off holding his Cloak of the Bat, and flew away — only to get hit by a fireball from the rival party
Chaos Peak
5
8
The Sun Goes Out
Aramil secretly stole the golden eye from the snake idol, and the DM revealed this plunged all of Faerun into eternal darkness
Dramatic Peak
6
7.5
Aramil Gets Squeezed to -10
The giant constrictor snake grappled Aramil and dealt 22 damage, dropping him to negative 10 HP — the first character to go down
Combat
7
7.5
Everybody Gets Poisoned (14 Damage)
The toxic gas trap hit the entire party for 14 damage each, nearly wiping them, with multiple players announcing their desperately low HP totals
Combat
8
7
If the Men Find Out We Can Shapeshift
Lavender's iconic quote about Tika's shapeshifting abilities had the table erupting — later voted Quote of the Session
Energy Peak
9
6.5
Mirror Melts the Gorilla
Moor turned the thermal mirror onto the Gorallon, and it melted through the creature in spectacular fashion — "it smells delicious... like cooked steak"
Energy Peak
10
6
Nat 1 Sends Rivals Fleeing
During the final escape, one of the rival mages rolled a Nat 1 on the save against Aramil's Phantasmal Force, causing him to panic, grab his buddy, and flee
Dice Peak
World Reference

Campaign Codex

👤 NPCs
🧙
Fizzleblast
Gnome Patron MAJOR
Neurotic, cashmere-obsessed quest giver hiding in his safety bubble. Hired the party, provided gear, set up the perimeter, and accidentally posted the expedition on a public forum. Refuses to leave Fizzle Field.
Alive
👦
Niles
Human Intern SUPPORTING
Prepubescent red-haired intern. Dropped the loot bag. Found shaking at the end. Pack mule, personal assistant, and emotional punching bag for a gnome who can't remember his name.
Alive
👹
Tika
Imp Familiar SUPPORTING
Lavender's familiar. New Jersey accent. "Hey! Meow." Died to the snake. Got better. Flew invisible to spy on the rival party, retrieved the Zephyr Reliquary from a high shelf, and shapeshifted into a raven to scout ahead.
Alive (resummoned)
🌌
Jezebel
Genie Patron MINOR
Contained in Lavender's locket. Wind/air type. Will be freed eventually. Mentioned but never directly appeared during the session.
Unknown
⚔
Blackwater (Rival Party)
Mercenaries SUPPORTING
Competing adventurers who found the expedition on Fizzleblast's public forum post. Entered from the roof, lost three members on the way in. Broke the mirrors, woke the T-Rex, launched a fireball at Bob, and got killed for it. Preferred to let a dinosaur do their dirty work.
Deceased
🐲
The T-Rex
Dinosaur MINOR
Temple canyon guardian. Sleeping when they arrived. The rivals' mirror-smashing plan fixed that. Bob stole the idol from above its head while it slept, then it woke up and chased the entire party down the canyon.
Alive
∾
Giant Constrictor Snake
Temple Guardian MINOR
40-foot snake that lived inside the statues. Woke up when Lavender took the Zephyr Reliquary. Killed Tika, squeezed Aramil to -10 HP, and got ended by Opal's blind critical hit for 30 damage.
Defeated
☣
Gorallons
Jungle Monstrosities MINOR
Four-armed jungle apes. Attacked on arrival. The welcome committee nobody asked for. Moor melted one with a redirected thermal mirror beam — "it smells delicious... like cooked steak."
Defeated
♥
Charisma Company
The Party MAJOR
Five adventurers, one shared brain cell, and a cashmere-funded expedition into eternal darkness. Moor, Aramil, Opal, Lavender, and Bob — hired by Fizzleblast to retrieve the Golden Snake Idol from the Temple of Man and Crocodile.
Active
📍 Locations
🏛
Temple of Man & Crocodile
Dungeon
Solar-powered death trap in the Chultan jungle. Mirror puzzles, pressure plates, giant snakes, and a sleeping T-Rex. Heliographic thermal arrays focus sunlight into flesh-melting beams.
Hostile Territory
🏝
The Shore / Fizzle Field
Safe Zone
Beach landing zone. Protected by Fizzleblast's magic bubble that repels undead. Black sand, screaming jungle, and a gnome sleeping through the apocalypse.
Safe Zone
🏫
Royal Community College
Institution
Aramil's former employer. He was "Adjunct Professor Emeritus." Left on a "sabbatical." Referenced in backstory only.
Referenced Only
🏙
Port Nyanzaru
City
Dinosaur races, merchant princes, and the persistent smell of jungle rot. Last taste of civilization before the Chultan interior. Where they rented the canoes.
Active
💎 Artifacts
🐍
Golden Snake Idol
Quest Objective
The quest objective. Connected to the sun. Removing it... had consequences. A golden serpent coiled around a sun disk, its jeweled eyes catching the last light of a dying sun.
Owner: Bob
👁
Eye of the Serpent
Cursed Gem — 5,000 GP
Gem pried from the idol by Aramil. Worth 5,000 gold. Warm as a coal that does not burn. May have caused the eternal darkness engulfing Faerun. Nobody else knows he took it.
Owner: Aramil (secret)
🌀
Zephyr Reliquary
Wondrous Item
Temple's "AC unit." Grants fire immunity. Removing it woke the giant snake. Retrieved by Tika from a high shelf. Now Lavender can't be burned.
Owner: Lavender
⚖
Scales of Justice
Artifact
Crocodile pedestal artifact. Re-roll one die per day. Bob swapped it off the pedestal Indiana Jones style — weight-for-weight, idol for scales.
Owner: Moor
⚜
Staff of Healing
Wondrous Item
Temple loot. Because the cleric wasn't already doing enough. The single most impactful item — without it, Moor couldn't have sustained 148 HP of total healing.
Owner: Moor
💪
Belt of Hill Giant Strength
Wondrous Item
Sets STR to 21. As if the barbarian needed more reasons to hit things harder. Bob found it for Opal despite his "just here for the loot" persona.
Owner: Opal
🦇
Cloak of the Bat
Wondrous Item
Advantage on stealth, flying in dim light. Perfect for the rogue who was already impossible to find. Enabled the Batman glide escape from the T-Rex canyon while carrying the idol.
Owner: Bob
Roll Credits

End Credits

Charisma Company: Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip • The Sunless Shore

Charisma Company: Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip

The Sunless Shore

The sun will not rise again in Faerun.

Cast
Kyle Moor Dragonborn Cleric (Life Domain), Level 3
Shannon Opal Goliath Barbarian, Level 3
Kelsey Lavender Tiefling Warlock (Genie Patron), Level 3
Josh Bob Halfling Rogue (Thief), Level 3
Kyle Aramil Half-Elf Warlock (Pact of the Chain), Level 3
Dungeon Master
Blue Orbit (Brandon)
Blue Orbit (Brandon)
By the Numbers
Encounters 4 (Gorallons, Giant Constrictor Snake, Toxic Gas Trap, Rival Party Ambush)
Nat 20s 7 (Opal 4, Bob 3) / Nat 1s: 1 (Aramil, treasure search)
Most Damage (Single Hit) Opal -- 30 damage, blind critical hit on the Giant Snake
Total Party Damage Taken 171 HP
Total Healing Done 148 HP (all by Moor, obviously)
Characters Downed 1 (Aramil -- crushed by snake, -10 HP)
Leeroy Jenkins Moments 5
Best Line "If the men find out we can shapeshift, they're going to tell the church." -- Lavender
Session Duration ~4 hours
Worlds Plunged Into Eternal Darkness 1
Special Thanks
  • Niles, for dropping the Bag of Holding (You beautiful disaster)
  • The Pterodactyls, for not screeching at the wrong moment
  • Fizzleblast's cashmere robe, for surviving the humidity
  • And to the T-Rex, for being a very heavy sleeper
CHARISMA COMPANY
Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip: "The Sunless Shore"

How MVP Works
Plot Impact (25%)
Driving the story forward through decisions, discoveries, or key actions. Bob's Bob was the MVP of the actual heist (10/10); Aramil's While Bob secured the mission success, Aramil drove the world plot (9/10).
Combat (20%)
Impact in combat encounters — damage, tactics, clutch saves. Opal's A classic barbarian performance (9/10); Moor's Moor was the MVP of survival (9/10).
Roleplay (25%)
Quality and commitment to in-character portrayal. Lavender's Kelsey fully embodied Lavender's "fabulous warlock" persona (9/10); Opal's Shannon turned a debilitating mechanical penalty (blindness) into the session's best running gag and emotional hook (9/10).
Teamwork (15%)
Supporting other players, enabling combos, sharing spotlight. Moor's The definition of a support player (10/10); Lavender's Lavender was the glue holding the party together (9/10).
Clutch Moments (15%)
Creating moments the table will remember and retell. Bob's Bob had the coolest action-movie sequences of the session (9/10); Lavender's Her reactions were consistently entertaining, and her combat prowess provided one of the most cinematic kills of the night (8/10).
Final score is a weighted average across all 5 categories. Weights: Plot Impact 25%, Combat 20%, Roleplay 25%, Teamwork 15%, Clutch Moments 15%. Tap any player row to see their full breakdown.

Physically executed the scale puzzle swap and the T-Rex heist to secure the win.
⚔

Moor (Kyle 1)
Moor's *Turn Undead* and *Mass Cure Wounds* saved the party; Opal's critical hit ended the boss fight.
🎭
Roleplay Royalty
Lavender (Kelsey)
Lavender's consistent fabulous persona and Opal turning blindness into the session's best ongoing arc.
🤝
Team Player
Moor (Kyle 1)
Kept the party alive through gas, fire, and snakes with selfless resource use.
🌟
Moment Maker
Bob (Josh)
The ceiling run, the *Indiana Jones* swap, and the final flight were cinematic gold.
🏆
Session MVP
Lavender (Kelsey)
The intellectual and social leader who solved the puzzles, managed the party, and blasted the enemies.

Combat Highlights
⚠
Beach Ambush (Girallons)
Medium (Environmental kills utilized)2 Rounds
2x Girallons
Moor utilized the thermal array mirrors to incinerate the first Girallon instantly. Aramil used *Speak with Animals* and a high Intimidation roll to force the second Girallon to flee, ending the combat without further resource expenditure.
💀
The Gas Chamber (Skeletons)
Easy (Solved via Channel Divinity)2 Rounds
9x Skeletons
Moor used *Turn Undead* (Destroy Undead) to instantly obliterate seven skeletons. The party took significant damage from the room's blade traps and gas before Lavender dispersed the toxins with *Gust of Wind*.
🐍
The Giant Constrictor Snake
Deadly (One PC downed)4 Rounds
1x Giant Constrictor Snake (Boss)
A brutal close-quarters fight. Aramil was constricted and dropped to 0 HP instantly. Bob provided consistent chip damage, but Opal ended the fight with a massive critical hit of 30 damage, cleaving the snake in half. Moor’s healing kept the party from a death state.
🚶
The T-Rex Escape
Medium (Tactical retreat)3 Rounds
T-Rex, Baboons, Rival Mages
The party focused on movement. Lavender accidentally hit Moor with a friendly-fire *Eldritch Blast* during a fumble. Bob successfully kited a pack of baboons while flying. During the escape through the antechamber, Aramil and Opal stumbled on pressure plates, each taking 13 damage from the erupting blade traps. Bob sailed overhead on enchanted boots, bypassing the traps entirely. The encounter ended when the party reached the safe zone as rival mages were devoured.

Key Findings
The Healer's MVP Status:
Moor utilized the *Staff of Healing* and Life Cleric features with near-perfect timing, effectively resetting the party's health pool twice.
The "Trap-Heavy" Toll:
Over 60% of total party damage came from environmental traps (Gas, Scythes, Heat) rather than direct monster attacks.
The Glass Cannon:
Aramil absorbed nearly 30% of the party's damage and was the only character to be downed, highlighting the vulnerability of the Warlock when targeted by high-CR monsters like the Giant Snake.

Lifting mechanisms and moving heavy statues
Luck Deviation Over Time
Cumulative deviation from the expected average (10.5). Above zero = rolling lucky, below = unlucky.
SESSION LUCK NARRATIVE
The dice gods clearly favored the party's rogue and barbarian, with Bob and Opal both maintaining averages nearly 5 points above the statistical norm. Bob's three nat 20s and clutch fireball dodge ensured his survival while carrying the heavy idol. Opal's four natural 20s turned potentially lethal encounters into mere roadblocks, particularly her crit on the giant snake. Moor rolled quietly but effectively, averaging 14.75 with three separate 18s across Strength and Sleight of Hand checks. Lavender's luck was a rollercoaster, swinging between 19s on initiative and raven attacks and brutal 3s on constitution saves and coordination. Meanwhile, Aramil suffered the session's most thematic failure, rolling a natural 1 while searching for treasure, and his cumulative deviation spent most of the session below zero.

Combat Replay Timeline

4 Encounters | 8 Rounds | 28 Turns

Encounters
8

Encounter 1
Gorallon Ambush at the Temple Entrance
VICTORY
▼
1
Rounds
9
Party Dmg
0
Crits
0
Downed
2
Enemies Killed
Two Gorallons (jungle apes) attacked the party at the temple entrance. Moor melted Gorallon 1 with a redirected thermal mirror. Lavender hit Gorallon 2 with Eldritch Blast for 9 damage, Aramil intimidated it into fleeing with Speak with Animals.
ROUND 1 9 dmgMVP: Moor-- Gorallon 1 (killed by mirror), Gorallon 2 (fled)
Moor Interact Redirect Thermal Mirror (Strength Check 18) > Gorallon 1
Creative use of the thermal mirror defense system to incinerate the Gorallon without a single attack roll. DM described it as 'cooked steak plus burnt hair.'
Gorallon 1 Death/Downed Killed by redirected thermal mirror beam KILLED
Burned alive by concentrated sunlight -- hole burned directly through its body
Lavender Attack Eldritch Blast (with Genie's Wrath thunder damage) > Gorallon 2 9 dmg
7 force + 2 thunder from Genie patron
Opal Interact Attempt to move mirror (failed Strength DC 18, then Raged for advantage) > Gorallon 2 (attempted)
Raged as bonus action for advantage on Strength, still failed initially. Eventually moved it with Bob's help on a natural 20.
+ Rage
Aramil Spell (Control) Speak with Animals + Intimidation (with advantage, rolled 18+4=22) > Gorallon 2
First Intimidation roll was a Nat 1 (rerolled due to advantage from speaking beast language). Second roll: 18+4=22. The Gorallon completely changed demeanor and bolted.
Gorallon 2 Movement Fled into the jungle (frightened by Aramil's intimidation)
Took 9 damage from Lavender's Eldritch Blast but fled rather than dying
Moor redirected a thermal mirror to incinerate Gorallon 1. Lavender hit Gorallon 2 with Eldritch Blast for 9 damage. Opal attempted to move a mirror (failed then succeeded with help). Aramil used Speak with Animals and Intimidation (22) to frighten Gorallon 2 into fleeing.
Encounter 2
Giant Constrictor Snake in the Crystal Prism Room
VICTORY
▼
3
Rounds
67
Party Dmg
1
Crits
1
Downed
1
Enemies Killed
A Giant Constrictor Snake (Vipoa variant with venomous bite) emerged from a snake statue's mouth. The party fought it over 3 rounds. The snake downed Aramil with a devastating 22-damage constriction, killed Tika the familiar, but Opal finished it with a critical hit Greataxe for 30 damage. Moor revived Aramil with Cure Wounds.
ROUND 1 29 dmgMVP: Bob
Aramil Attack Eldritch Blast > Giant Constrictor Snake
Blast sailed right over the snake's head
Opal Attack Greataxe Attack > Giant Constrictor Snake
Snake AC is 14 -- the snake dodged the axe despite its size
Aramil Attack Eldritch Blast (second attempt, from earlier initiative block) > Giant Constrictor Snake 6 dmg
Tika (Raven form) Attack Beak Attack (Raven) > Giant Constrictor Snake 7 dmg
Tika gouged out one of the snake's eyes -- disadvantage on attacks implied
Moor Spell (Damage) Toll the Dead (cantrip, Wisdom save DC 15) > Giant Constrictor Snake 8 dmg
Snake failed its Wisdom save. d12 damage because the snake was already damaged.
Bob Attack Shortbow Attack with Sneak Attack (Opal adjacent) > Giant Constrictor Snake 8 dmg
Snake now bloodied (half HP). Applied Vex for advantage on next attack.
Opening salvo of attacks. Aramil and Opal both missed (9 and 12 vs AC 14). Aramil's second Eldritch Blast connected for 6 damage. Tika gouged out an eye for 7 damage. Moor's Toll the Dead dealt 8 necrotic, and Bob's Sneak Attack shortbow added 8 more. Snake bloodied.
ROUND 2 8 dmgMVP: Moor
Giant Constrictor Snake Attack Bite Attack (with venom) on Tika > Tika (Raven/Imp) 5 dmg
Lavender used Investment of the Chain Master reaction to grant Tika resistance to the damage. Tika failed Con save (7) and became paralyzed.
Giant Constrictor Snake Attack Constrict (Multiattack second attack) on Opal > Opal
Opal's Athletics/Strength save was 16+8=24 (Belt of Hill Giant Strength). She flexed so hard the snake could not wrap around her.
Bob Attack Shortbow Attack with Sneak Attack (advantage from Vex) > Giant Constrictor Snake 8 dmg
Terrible Sneak Attack damage rolls again (1, 1, 2 on d6s). Bob lamented his dice.
Opal Attack Greataxe Attack > Giant Constrictor Snake
Missed again. AC 14 is proving tough for Opal.
Lavender Attack Eldritch Blast > Giant Constrictor Snake
Damage not clearly stated in transcript for this specific hit.
Moor Spell (Heal) Cure Wounds (via Staff of Healing, 1 charge) on Opal > Opal
Healed Opal for 2d8+3 = 14 HP via the Staff of Healing
Snake struck back -- bit and paralyzed Tika, tried to constrict Opal but her 24 Strength save held firm. Bob hit for another 8. Opal and others missed. Moor healed Opal for 14 HP via Staff of Healing.
ROUND 3 52 dmgMVP: Opal-- Tika (familiar destroyed), Aramil (downed to 0 HP, revived), Giant Constrictor Snake (killed)
Giant Constrictor Snake Attack Bite (auto-crit on paralyzed Tika) + Constrict on Aramil > Tika, then Aramil CRIT!
Auto-crit on paralyzed Tika destroyed the familiar (it disappears as a fey creature). Then grappled Aramil who rolled only a 5 on Athletics.
Tika (Imp/Raven) Death/Downed Destroyed by auto-crit bite while paralyzed
Familiar destroyed -- requires 10-minute ritual to resummon
Giant Constrictor Snake Attack Constrict (squeeze damage, 3d8+4) > Aramil 22 dmg
22 damage dropped Aramil to -10 HP (unconscious at 0). Most devastating single hit of the session.
Aramil Death/Downed Downed by snake constriction (0 HP)
First (and only) PC downed during the entire session
Opal Attack Greataxe Attack -- CRITICAL HIT (Natural 20) > Giant Constrictor Snake 30 dmgCRIT!
CRITICAL HIT! First crit of the session! 2d12+6 = 12+12+6 = 30 damage! Killed the snake! Opal chopped through the coiled snake 'like a cake but less delicately.' Previously blinded by the mirror beam, proving even temporary setbacks can't stop a Goliath.
Giant Constrictor Snake Death/Downed Killed by Opal's critical hit Greataxe KILLED
Chopped through multiple coiled layers while wrapped around Aramil
Moor Spell (Heal) Cure Wounds on Aramil (reviving from 0 HP) > Aramil
Brought Aramil back from 0 HP to 15 HP. Clutch healing.
The climactic round. Snake auto-critted paralyzed Tika (destroying the familiar), then constricted Aramil for 22 damage, dropping him to 0 HP. Opal answered with a NATURAL 20 CRIT on her Greataxe for 30 damage, killing the snake! Moor revived Aramil with Cure Wounds to 15 HP.
Encounter 3
Baboon Swarm Skirmish
VICTORY
▼
2
Rounds
24
Party Dmg
0
Crits
0
Downed
5
Enemies Killed
Feral baboons and injured Gorallons dropped into the rooms from the roof as darkness fell. The party fought while solving puzzles. Aramil killed a baboon with Eldritch Blast, Lavender accidentally hit Moor with a Nat 1 Eldritch Blast (5 friendly fire), then killed 2 baboons with Hellish Rebuke. Bob distracted the rest by screaming and running, then hiding on the ceiling.
ROUND 1 24 dmgMVP: Lavender-- 3 baboons killed
Moor Spell (Buff) Shield of Faith on Lavender (+2 AC) > Lavender
Aramil Attack Eldritch Blast > Baboon 9 dmg
Killed the baboon outright -- they are very fragile
Lavender Attack Eldritch Blast -- CRITICAL FAIL (Nat 1, hit Moor for friendly fire) > Baboon (intended) / Moor (actual) 5 dmg
Nat 1! A baboon jumped on Lavender as she was casting, and she blasted Moor in the face for 5 force damage. Friendly fire!
Baboon Pack Attack Bite Attack (Pack Tactics, advantage) on Lavender > Lavender 5 dmg
1d4+2 = 5 damage. Triggered Lavender's Hellish Rebuke reaction.
Lavender Spell (Damage) Hellish Rebuke (Reaction, 2d10 fire damage) > Baboon (2 killed by fire spread) 10 dmg
Rolled 10 on 2d10, baboon saved (DC 14 met) for half = 5 damage, but enough to kill it. The volatile heat caused the fire to spread to an adjacent baboon, killing both!
Moor buffed Lavender with Shield of Faith. Aramil killed a baboon with Eldritch Blast (9 damage). Lavender rolled a Nat 1 and blasted Moor for 5 friendly fire. A baboon bit Lavender for 5 damage, triggering Hellish Rebuke which killed 2 baboons via fire spread.
ROUND 2 MVP: Bob-- Remaining baboons (killed by spike traps)
Bob Other Distraction -- screaming and running to lure baboons, then Stealth on ceiling (27) > Baboon Pack
Bob screamed and ran to draw the baboons into the spike trap room, then used Spider Climbing Slippers to run up the wall and ceiling. Stealth check: 19+8=27. The baboons ran into the spike traps.
Bob executed a brilliant distraction, screaming and running to lure the remaining baboons into the spike trap room, then hiding on the ceiling with a 27 Stealth check.
Encounter 4
Escape and Rival Party Ambush
CONTAINED
▼
2
Rounds
0
Party Dmg
0
Crits
0
Downed
0
Enemies Killed
During the final timed escape (4 minutes 20 seconds), a rival party of mages attacked. Bob was hit by a Fireball for 9 damage (half on Dex save 24). Two rival mages Dimension Doored to block the exit. Moor's Command ('Flee') was saved against, but Aramil's Phantasmal Force caused a Nat 1 Intelligence save, making both mages flee. The party barely reached safety.
ROUND 1 9 dmgMVP: Bob
Rival Caster (rooftop) Spell (Damage) Fireball (targeted at Bob) > Bob 9 dmg
Bob rolled a 24 Dex save, taking half of ~18 damage = 9. The T-Rex took the full Fireball and woke up furious, triggering a stampede of all creatures in the area.
A rival caster on the rooftop launched a Fireball at Bob as he flew away with the idol. Bob saved (Dex 24) for half damage (9). The Fireball hit the sleeping T-Rex, waking it and triggering a stampede.
ROUND 2 MVP: Aramil
Rival Mage 1 Spell (Control) Dimension Door (teleported to block party exit) > Party (blocking)
Two rival mages Dimension Doored directly in front of the party at the temple exit
Moor Spell (Control) Command ('Flee') -- target saved (Wisdom save vs DC 12+) > Rival Mage 1
The rival mage made its Wisdom save. Command failed.
Aramil Spell (Control) Phantasmal Force (illusion of raptors chasing) -- target rolled Nat 1 on Intelligence save > Rival Mage 2
NAT 1 on the Intelligence save! The rival mage panicked from the raptor illusion, grabbed his buddy (who had saved against Command), and BOTH fled! Encounter-ending spell!
Rival Mage 1 Movement Fled (dragged by panicking Rival Mage 2)
Dragged away by his panicking companion
Rival Mage 2 Movement Fled (Phantasmal Force -- believed raptors were chasing)
Nat 1 on Intelligence save against Phantasmal Force
Two rival mages Dimension Doored to block the exit. Moor's Command ('Flee') failed. But Aramil's Phantasmal Force caused a Nat 1 Intelligence save -- the second mage grabbed his buddy and both fled, clearing the path. The party sprinted into Fizzleblast's safety bubble.
Session Pacing

Session Pacing Stats

~4h Runtime | 9 Scenes | Pace: Late Bloomer

9

Avg Scene
Peak 10/10
The Escape
— Scene 9, Frantic, high-energy, rapid-fire rolling.
Engagement Waveform
How energy rose and fell across the session. Hover dots for details, click to inspect.
Click a data point to see details
Time by Scene Type
Where the session spent its time. Click a bar to filter scenes below.
What Worked
The "Ticking Clock" Mechanic:
The DM tracked in-game time (sunset/heat rising) and rolled for consequences every 10 minutes. This kept the players moving even when they wanted to dither. The consequence of the "heat rising" forcing damage was excellent motivation.
The Climactic Skill Challenge:
Transitioning from the T-Rex stealth encounter directly into a high-speed chase/escape was brilliant. It prevented a slog of a combat against a T-Rex (which might have TPK'd them) and turned it into a cinematic exit.
Physics-Based Puzzles:
Allowing players to solve the pressure plate puzzle by piling loot/gold rather than just rolling dice made for an engaging, tactile scene.
What Dragged
Loot Logistics:
There were two distinct phases (Scene 2 and Scene 6) where the game stopped for loot distribution. The "random bag" mechanic is fun in theory but slow in practice as players have to look up what items do, figure out attunement, and calculate carrying capacity.
Post-Break Momentum:
Returning from the break directly into individual "10-minute looting/investigation" turns killed the table momentum. It isolated players (Bob sneaking, Lavender investigating) rather than bringing the party back together.
Carrying Capacity Math:
The debate about dropping a crossbow vs. rations to fit gold items took up valuable time that could have been spent on the adventure.

Character Development

Growth Report Cards

📚 Character development tracked across the session. Grades reflect observable growth, not player skill — an "F" in Moral Integrity means a fascinating villain arc, not a bad player.
Moor
→ Stabilizing — GPA 3.71
Courage: A Teamwork: A M. Integrity: A Problem-Solving: A Consistency: A MVP Moments: B Emotional Growth: C
"Moor, I want you to know that I see what you do when you think no one is watching. I see you testing the pressure plate with your mace before anyone else steps forward. I see you casting Aid on three people and not including yourself. I see you standing behind Opal with Guidance ready the moment she needs it, never asking for credit, never even mentioning it. You healed 148 hit points this session. Every single point of recovery this party received came from your hands, your spells, your staff. And when Aramil was at negative ten, wrapped in a giant constrictor snake, you didn't hesitate -- "I got him." Two words. That is the most Moor sentence in the world, and it might be my favorite thing anyone said all night. What makes you remarkable isn't just your tactical mind -- though that mirror gambit in the first room was genuinely inspired. It's that you lead without needing to be seen leading. "Run through door. Why use many word when few word do trick?" You made an entire table of overthinkers move with three words. That's not stoicism. That's authority. The one thing I'd gently push you on: let them see what's behind the efficiency sometimes. When you healed Opal's blindness, there was a moment -- just a flicker -- where the cleric of Tyr was being kind, not just effective. More of that. The party trusts you with their hit points. I think they'd trust you with a full sentence, too. You are the foundation this party was built on. And foundations don't get enough credit. So here it is: thank you, Moor. Well done."
Opal
↗ Awakening — GPA 3.43
Courage: A Teamwork: A Consistency: A M. Integrity: B Emotional Growth: B MVP Moments: B Problem-Solving: C
"Opal, you walked into this temple like you walk into everything -- arms open, heart first, ready to be delighted. "I'm delighted to be here. I'm delighted by everyone." And you know what? That delight is your superpower. It's easy to be brave when you're calculating the odds. It's something else entirely to be brave because you're too excited to be scared. But the moment that made me proudest wasn't the critical hit on the snake -- though that was spectacular, and I want you to know that killing a giant constrictor while recovering from blindness is not something most adventurers can claim. No, the moment that mattered most was when you said "Someone's gotta tell me where to go and what to do." That was Opal -- strongest person in the room -- admitting she needed help. And then holding Lavender's hand and saying "This is very nice, thank you." You have a heart the size of your great axe, Opal. The way you immediately adopted Bob ("I'm like, ah" when he was between your legs moving the mirror), the way you called Moor "Dragon" with such affection when he healed your eyes, the way you were genuinely worried about everyone's health during the escape -- you care so much, and you don't even try to hide it. Next time, trust your instincts a little more in puzzles. You see more than you think you do. And keep swinging that axe like you mean it -- because you always do."
Aramil
↓ Fracturing — GPA 2.43
Consistency: A Courage: B Problem-Solving: B MVP Moments: B Emotional Growth: C Teamwork: D M. Integrity: F
"Aramil. I've been thinking about what to say to you, and I keep coming back to that moment in the hallway when you told the others about your "sabbatical." "It was definitely my choice to leave." The way you said it -- practiced, defensive, daring anyone to challenge the lie. And then, quieter: "Maybe my dad finally noticed me." You are, without question, the most intelligent person in this party. The gorillon diplomacy was masterful. "Think about your wife and kids" -- said in the beast's own language, with advantage, rolling a 22. That's not just cleverness. That's someone who understands how others think and what they fear. You could use that gift to build something extraordinary. Instead, you used it to steal a golden eye from a snake idol and plunge an entire continent into eternal darkness. I'm not going to pretend I'm not disappointed. You read the murals. You understood the warning. You looked at the consequences -- the undead rising, the sun going dark, the world falling apart -- and you said "Cool. I'm cool with that." Because a community college might give you your office back. But here's what I need you to hear: the fact that you knew what you were doing makes you more interesting, not less. You didn't steal that eye out of ignorance. You stole it because the hole your father left in you is so deep that you'd fill it with the world's light if you could. That's not evil. That's grief wearing a fedora. I hope you find what you're looking for, Aramil. I suspect the eye isn't it. But the search? The search is one of the most compelling stories at this table. Keep searching. Just... maybe check the murals next time before you commit to the apocalypse."
Lavender
↑ Ascending — GPA 3.86
Courage: A Teamwork: A Emotional Growth: A Problem-Solving: A Consistency: A MVP Moments: A M. Integrity: B
"Lavender, you started this session by saying "I don't know how to play D&D." I want you to remember that, because by the end of this session, you were the MVP, and it wasn't even close. What happened in between was one of the most satisfying character arcs I've ever witnessed in a single session. You went from uncertain and deferring to decisive, creative, and essential. When the poison gas hit and everyone was panicking, you cast Gust of Wind. When the baboons attacked, you Hellish Rebuked one so hard the fire spread to its neighbor. When the snake statues needed moving and everyone else would burn, you said "I'll do it" -- because you were the only one who could. But the thing that makes you special isn't the spells. It's the way you treated Opal when she went blind. You gave her a bandana. You booped her on the head. You held her hand. In a session full of thermal beams and T-Rexes and poison gas, you stopped to be kind to someone who was scared. That's not tactics. That's character. "If the men find out we can shapeshift, they're going to tell the church." That line made an entire table lose it, and it's the perfect distillation of who you are: sharp, surprising, a little bit wicked, and absolutely delightful. Your relationship with Tika is genuine ("She's my butt"), your trust in Jezebel is beautiful, and your willingness to offer the Genie Vessel to save the whole party -- knowing it was your one-per-day escape -- shows a generosity that your sarcasm tries very hard to hide. You're not hiding anymore, Lavender. And the table is better for it."
Bob
↑ Ascending — GPA 3.43
Courage: A Problem-Solving: A Consistency: A MVP Moments: A Teamwork: B Emotional Growth: B M. Integrity: C
"Bob. Or should I say, "just Bob." The kid who showed up on the first day with his hood pulled so deep you could barely see his eyes, wrote his own name on his hand in case he forgot it, and told us he was "just here for the loot, baby." And then you screamed down a hallway to draw a pack of feral baboons away from your friends. That's the thing about you, Bob. You perform distance -- the hood, the sarcasm, the minimal information. "I'm, uh... Married." "This fucking guy." You want everyone to think you're an island. And then you spend your search time finding a Belt of Hill Giant Strength for Shannon and a cape for Kyle, and you draw a stick-figure diagram on the ground to explain your Spider Slipper plan to Opal because she didn't understand the words. The Batman moment over the T-Rex was objectively the highlight of the session, and you earned it. Climbing in total darkness, hanging by your legs, Sleight of Hand 22 on the idol, flying out on a cape while a fireball explodes behind you. That is the kind of moment that D&D was built for. And it happened because a halfling rogue who said he was "just here for the loot" decided to be the one who grabbed it. But what I'll remember most is the baboon hallway. You looked at the party and said "start screaming and making as much noise as I can running down the hallway." Then stealth 27, up the ceiling, silent. That's not a thief. That's someone who cares more than he'd ever admit. The night, indeed, Bob. The night indeed."
Class Valedictorian

Pair Analysis
Moor & Lavender
The Brain Trust
These two were the primary mechanical problem solvers for the puzzles. Their dialogue was constant, focusing on the "how-to" of the mirrors and pressure plates.
Best Moment
Moor suggests using weights on pressure plates; Lavender immediately offers her heavy shield to help find the "threshold."
Combat Synergy
Moor casts *Shield of Faith* on Lavender while she moves to distract the T-Rex.
22exchanges
4yes-and
2banter
3combat
3RP
Opal & Lavender
Loyal Companions
This pair provided the strongest emotional heart of the session. The "Big Sister/Little Sister" dynamic between the tiny Tiefling and the massive Goliath was a highlight.
Best Moment
When Opal is blinded, Lavender gives her a "pretty bandana" and a "boop on the head" to comfort the Goliath.
18exchanges
3yes-and
4banter
2combat
2RP
Moor & Opal
Battle Siblings
They operated as the "front line" and "the muscle," coordinating physical tasks and health management.
Combat Synergy
Moor uses the *Staff of Healing* to specifically clear Opal’s blindness so she can help with the final puzzle.
16exchanges
2yes-and
4combat
Opal & Bob
Comic Relief Duo
Their interactions were almost entirely based on their extreme size difference (Goliath vs. Halfling), providing consistent table laughs.
14exchanges
3banter
Aramil & Bob
New Acquaintances
Lowest direct interaction count. They mostly operated independently (Bob sneaking/Aramil investigating), though they synchronized well for the final sprint.

Comparing their stealth/flight capabilities for the final "yoink" of the idol.

Group Dynamics
Core Bond Moor ↔ Lavender ↔ Opal. This triangle formed the functional engine of the session, handling heali...
Social Connector Lavender. She had meaningful interactions with every member, notably bridging the gap between the...
Dramatic Engine Moor ↔ Lavender. Their shared spellcaster logic and the friendly-fire incident provided the most ...
Comedy Team Opal ↔ Bob. The physical comedy of the Goliath and the Halfling was a recurring theme.
Support Harmony: Moor (the Cleric) and Lavender (the Warlock) show zero competition for the "leader" role; they build on each other's ...
Physicality as RP: Opal and Bob used their character sizes (Goliath vs. Halfling) to dictate their chemistry more than their backstories.
The "Skill Challenge" Glue: The high-stress escape at the end was the first time Bob and Aramil truly integrated with the main social group, sugg...
Growth Opportunities
Aramil ↔ Moor: As the two "older" or more "professional" characters (Professor vs. Cleric), they have room for a...

Psychological Analysis

Personality Profiles

Psychological Shadow Analysis

Dark Triad Analysis

Narcissism, Machiavellianism, and Psychopathy traits scored from observed in-session behavior and dialogue.
Moor
Kyle 1
Dragonborn Cleric (Life Domain)
Trait
Severity
Score
Narcissism
2
3
Machiavel.
3
4
Psychopathy
2
3
Total Score
10 / 30
Profile Analysis
On the surface, Moor's silence and efficiency read as pure duty. But his economy of speech is also an economy of empathy — he heals with clinical precision, not emotional engagement. His terse directives function as a control mechanism over a chaotic group, and his selective allocation of healing resources is, structurally, a leverage system that he operates without anyone noticing.
Behavioral Pattern
Clinical Altruist
Companion Note
Deeper analysis of Moor's profile finds a consistent pattern: caretaking that is real but mechanistic, authority that is earned but unquestioned, and an emotional architecture that processes distress as a logistics problem rather than an emotional event. He is the party's most genuinely good character — but his goodness operates through efficiency rather than empathy, which is a distinction worth noting.
Opal
Shannon
Goliath Barbarian
Trait
Severity
Score
Narcissism
2
2
Machiavel.
1
1
Psychopathy
3
4
Total Score
7 / 30
Profile Analysis
It largely does not. Opal is the party's most genuinely clean profile. Close scrutiny finds one narrow case for a single-point psychopathy bump and nothing else. Honest analysis confirms rather than undermines Opal's fundamental warmth.
Behavioral Pattern
Joyful Combatant
Companion Note
Close scrutiny of Opal's profile confirms her cleanness. She is the party's emotional baseline — warm, trusting, transparent, and genuinely good. The one-point psychopathy bump reflects a real but minor pattern of enthusiasm for violence and a self-described emotional simplicity. She is not dark. She is occasionally very happy to chop things, and the analysis notes this without alarm.
Aramil
Kyle 2
Half-Elf Warlock (Archaeologist)
Trait
Severity
Score
Narcissism
5
7
Machiavel.
7
8
Psychopathy
4
6
Total Score
21 / 30
Profile Analysis
Aramil's scores are already the party's highest, and the evidence doesn't require inventing darkness — only refusing to explain it away. A generous reading might credit his Speak with Animals as "creative problem-solving" and his mural investigation as "intellectual curiosity." But every social interaction Aramil initiated was manipulative, every piece of knowledge he acquired was hoarded for personal use, and his single most consequential decision was an act of world-ending selfishness performed in secret while his allies were running for their lives.
Behavioral Pattern
Wounded Academic with Predatory Instrumentalism
Companion Note
A generous framing would describe Aramil as "morally flexible" and "self-interested." The evidence shows that the flexibility has no floor and the self-interest has no ceiling. He executed a premeditated theft using information asymmetry, exploited his party's labor as the vehicle for his personal gain, weaponized empathy in every social interaction, and assessed a continent of suffering as an acceptable cost for a faculty position. He is the party's most functionally dark character by a significant margin — and the evidence is not subtle.
Lavender
Kelsey
Tiefling Warlock (Genie/Chain Pact)
Trait
Severity
Score
Narcissism
2
3
Machiavel.
3
4
Psychopathy
1
1
Total Score
8 / 30
Profile Analysis
Minimally. Lavender's profile is nearly as clean as Opal's, with one significant difference: Lavender is strategically competent in a way that, under close examination, reveals a mild pattern of influence management. The analysis finds two narrow single-point elevations and confirms the psychopathy floor.
Behavioral Pattern
Strategic Helper
Companion Note
Close analysis of Lavender finds two narrow single-point bumps — a self-deprecating competence awareness that functions as mild narcissistic presentation, and an intelligence network that gives her persistent informational advantage over her allies. Neither is dark in any meaningful sense. She is the party's most effective and most genuinely warm character. What the analysis adds is the observation that warmth and strategic competence coexist in Lavender, and the line between generosity and influence is thinner than it first appears.
Bob
Josh
Halfling Rogue
Trait
Severity
Score
Narcissism
3
4
Machiavel.
5
7
Psychopathy
4
6
Total Score
17 / 30
Profile Analysis
A surface reading treats Bob's sarcasm as a persona and his loot-focus as a harmless mercenary orientation. But Bob's "persona" is also his personality — a consistent operational mode in which people are evaluated by annoyance level, relationships are transactional, emotional disclosure is treated as a vulnerability, and the accumulation of resources is the only stated purpose for being present. "I'm just here for the loot, baby" is not a joke. It is a mission statement that was never updated, contradicted, or softened by any subsequent behavior.
Behavioral Pattern
Affectless Professional with Transactional Architecture
Companion Note
A surface description of Bob is "a quiet rogue with a dry sense of humor." Closer analysis reveals that the quietness is a consistent emotional absence, the dryness is a distancing mechanism, and the humor is deployed to maintain a barrier between Bob and genuine interpersonal connection. He is not malicious. He is not unkind. But he moved through an entire session of blindness, near-death, poison gas, and T-Rex encounters without once acknowledging that the people around him were having experiences — and the analysis finds that notable.

Other tracked analytics from this session
Party Chemistry Stats
PairExchangesChemistry ScoreChemistry Type
Moor <-> Lavender229/10Brain Trust
Opal <-> Lavender189/10Loyal Companions
Moor <-> Opal168/10Battle Siblings
Opal <-> Bob147/10Comic Relief Duo

Lavender <-> Bob126/10Odd Couple

Methodology:Tracked PC-to-PC exchanges, "Yes, And" moments, combat assists, and disagreements. Chemistry types assigned per pair.
Total PC-to-PC Exchanges:114
Social Connector:Lavender (meaningful interactions with every party member).
Core Bond Triangle:Moor <-> Lavender <-> Opal
Glass Cannon Rating
CharacterDamage DealtDamage TakenRatioTimes DownedGlass Cannon Rating
Opal30142.1:10Medium (Rage mitigates)
Bob2192.3:10Low (avoided combat)
Moor25+181.4:10Low (tank/healer)
Lavender150Inf:10Untouchable
Aramil0350:11Inverse Glass Cannon
Methodology:Ratio of damage dealt to damage taken. Higher ratio = more glass cannon. Includes an assessment of defensive choices.
Most Fragile:Aramil -- took the most damage (35 HP), dealt the least (0 combat damage), and was the only PC downed. A textbook glass cannon without the cannon.
Most Durable:Lavender -- took zero damage all session while contributing 15 damage and solving the key puzzle. The Zephyr Reliquary's fire immunity kept her completely safe.
Dice Luck Tracker
CharacterTotal RollsAverageDeviationNat 20sNat 1sLuck Rating
Bob1515.4+4.930Blessed by the Dice Gods
Opal1913.9+3.440Blessed by the Dice Gods
Aramil1210.75+0.2501Perfectly Average
Moor914.2+3.700Above Average (small sample)
Lavender811.3+0.800Slightly Above Average
Methodology:63 total d20 rolls extracted from transcript. Player averages compared against expected mean of 10.5.
Session Luck Narrative:The dice gods clearly favored Bob and Opal. Bob maintained a staggering 15.4 average across 15 rolls with three Natural 20s (Initiative, Dex save vs. Fireball, and one more). Opal's four Natural 20s turned potentially deadly encounters into highlights -- her Strength checks and boss-killing crit were all powered by exceptional luck. Aramil's perfectly average 10.75 made his session feel worse than it was, compounded by a Nat 1 on his loot search.
Luckiest Single Roll:Bob's Nat 20 Dex save dodging a Fireball while flying with the idol.
Unluckiest Single Roll:Aramil's Nat 1 searching for treasure -- found nothing in a room full of gold.

Act-by-Act Sentiment Timeline
Expand All
A1
Arrival & The Loot Sack Explosion
W1-3
Amusement (50%), Excitement (30%), Satisfaction (20%)
+4.0
▶
Fizzleblast's chaotic briefing and "squirtons" currency. Niles accidentally launching the Bag of Holding at the party. Random magical item distribution (instant attunement)
Maybe you're desperate, maybe you're hurting for a squirting, I don't know."
A2
The Burning Mirrors & Girallons
W4-5
Excitement (40%), Amusement (35%), Satisfaction (25%)
+5.5
▶
Girallon encounter at temple entrance. Moor melts a Girallon with the mirror beam. Aramil intimidates the second Girallon via Speak with Animals
Girallon encounter at temple entrance
A3
The Antechamber of Weights
W6-8
Satisfaction (35%), Excitement (35%), Amusement (20%), Concern (10%)
+5.0
▶
Pressure plate puzzle ("Man and Crocodile" mural). Moor's mace destroyed by spike trap. 10-minute looting sequence with incredible rolls
What if we weigh significantly different amounts? Like a halfling and a barbarian." -- Lavender
A4
The Prism Room & The Snake
W9-11
Tension (40%), Fear (25%), Excitement (20%), Satisfaction (15% -- at resolution)
-2.0
▶
Gas trap triggers: 14 damage to entire party, poison condition. Opal blinded by mirror beam (Nat 1). Giant Constrictor Snake emerges
I'm blind. Opal does not have dark vision." -- Opal
A5
Aligning the Serpent's Eyes
W12-13
Satisfaction (45%), Excitement (30%), Calm (25%)
+4.5
▶
Lavender uses fire immunity to walk through beams. Party collaborates on statue alignment. Bob/Moor pull off Indiana Jones swap for Scales of Justice
I can move this snake statue back, walk right through the beams, and I'd be saved... Want to try?" -- Lavender
A6
The Sleeping King & The Escape
W14
Excitement (50%), Tension (25%), Joy (25%)
+7.5
▶
T-Rex sleeping under heat lamps -- massive tension. Rival mercenaries overheard planning sabotage. Bob goes solo: flies up, grabs the idol
T-Rex sleeping under heat lamps -- massive tension
A7
The Bubble & The Blackout
W15
Joy (40% -- first half) -> Fear (30% -- twist) -> Amusement (30% -- meta-enjoyment of the twist)
+3.0
▶
Celebration in the safety bubble. Rivals devoured by undead outside. Aramil reveals he stole the Eye of the Serpent (Sleight of Hand 18)
I rolled an 18 on the sleight of hand to take it... I take the eye of the serpent." -- Aramil
Dominant Emotions
Amusement30%
Fizzleblast absurdity, squirtons, party banter
Excitement25%
Loot drops, heist sequence, chase
Tension20%
Snake fight, gas trap, T-Rex
Satisfaction15%
Puzzle solutions, mirror kills, teamwork
Fear10%
Darkness twist, near-death moments
Highest Positive Peaks
+9.0Bob's T-Rex Heist -- Solo flight, idol grab, Fireball dodge. Peak player agency
+7.0Opal's Blind Crit Kill -- Blinded barbarian crits the snake. Iconic
+7.0Belt of Giant Strength Drop -- Opal gets STR 23. Table erupts
+6.5Girallon Intimidation -- "Think about your wife and kids." Creative brilliance
+6.5Lavender's Fire Walk -- Fire immunity puzzle solution. MVP moment
Lowest Negative Points
-6.0Aramil Crushed to 0 HP -- Genuine table concern, snake constriction damage
-6.0DM's Apocalypse Narration -- "The sun will not rise again." Narrative despair
-4.0Gas Trap -- 14 damage to everyone, poison condition. "We're in trouble."
-4.0Opal Blinded -- Nat 1 on mirror push. Physical comedy meets real consequences
-3.0Tika Killed -- Lavender's familiar sacrificed. Emotional cost of survival
Emotional Signature: This session's emotional fingerprint is "The Heist Movie" -- extended setup with escalating competence, a dangerous middle act that tests the team, and a climactic sequence that delivers maximum payoff. The twist ending adds a layer of dark irony uncommon in one-shots, transforming a successful heist into the inadvertent cause of an apocalypse.
Per-Player Emotional Arcs
Individual sentiment trajectories across all windows. Click a name to toggle visibility.
Moor
The Steady Hand
+3.0
Mean
+6.0
Peak
-2.0
Valley
8.0
Range
Moor maintained consistent emotional stability throughout the session, rarely spiking above +5 or below -1. As the party healer, his engagement peaked during moments of crisis (Mass Cure Wounds saving the party) and during efficient problem-solving (mirror kill, pressure plate logic).
Peak: Window 14 (+6.0, Excitement) -- Skill challenge escape, party survival
Valley: Window 10 (-2.0, Tension) -- Aramil crushed to 0 HP, forced emergency healing
Aramil
The Rollercoaster
+1.8
Mean
+6.5
Peak
-6.0
Valley
12.5
Range
Aramil experienced the widest emotional swings of any player. His creative Girallon intimidation produced a high, but being crushed to 0 HP by the snake created the session's deepest personal valley.
Peak: Window 5 (+6.5, Joy) -- Intimidating Girallon with "think about your wife and kids"
Valley: Window 10 (-6.0, Fear) -- Crushed to 0 HP by the constrictor snake
Opal
The Reactor
+3.7
Mean
+7.0
Peak
-4.0
Valley
11.0
Range
Opal's emotional trajectory was defined by dramatic swings driven by physical comedy and combat extremes. Her blindness (Nat 1) produced the session's most memorable low, but her blind critical hit kill on the snake immediately after was the session's most celebrated moment.
Peak: Windows 8, 11, 14 (+7.0, Joy/Excitement) -- Belt of Giant Strength, blind snake kill, T-Rex escape
Valley: Window 9 (-4.0, Frustration) -- Blinded by mirror beam on a Nat 1
Lavender
The Arc
+3.6
Mean
+7.0
Peak
-3.0
Valley
10.0
Range
Lavender's arc tracked steadily upward as the session progressed. Initially observational and sarcastic, she became increasingly central to the party's success -- dispersing gas with Gust of Wind, sacrificing Tika, walking through fire beams, and scouting the T-Rex with her imp.
Peak: Window 14 (+7.0, Excitement) -- Imp scouting reveals rival plan, heist succeeds
Valley: Window 10 (-3.0, Concern) -- Tika killed by the snake, Aramil dying
Bob
The Enthusiast
+3.9
Mean
+9.0
Peak
-1.5
Valley
10.5
Range
Bob maintained consistently positive energy with a noticeable upward trend toward the session's climax. His Cloak of the Bat acquisition was a turning point, and his solo heist of the idol from the sleeping T-Rex was the session's crowning moment of player agency.
Peak: Window 14 (+9.0, Joy) -- Solo idol heist, flying escape from T-Rex, Fireball dodge
Valley: Window 10 (-1.5, Tension) -- Snake fight tension, teammate down
Decision Analytics

Leeroy Jenkins Index

#1
Moor
Calculated Risk-Taker
100%
2 Moments
▼
The Mirror Angle
The situation: The party just landed on the beach. Gorallons were charging. Everyone was panicking and asking "what do we do?"
What Moor did: Without waiting for initiative or a group plan, he manually pivoted a massive thermal mirror and aimed the focused sun-ray directly at the lead Gorallon.
Result: The Gorallon was instantly turned into cooked steak. Established the mirror mechanic as the party's primary weapon for the rest of the session.
Brilliant
The Scale Yoink
The situation: The party found a pedestal with a set of scales and a Tyr symbol. The DM was trying to explain the setup.
What Moor did: Explicitly ignored the DM's "Hang on, before you grab that--" and just said "No, I grab it."
Result: Triggered a room-wide collapse trap. But Bob pulled off a clutch swap with oil flasks to save the party. Moor walked away with the Scales of Justice. Forced Bob into a high-stakes swap to save the party.
Brilliant
#2
Bob
Calculated Heist
100%
1 Moment
▼
The Idol Grab
The situation: The party was escaping the temple. A golden idol sat on a pedestal in the T-Rex nest. Everyone else was running.
What Bob did: Broke from the group, used his Boots of Striding and Springing for a 36-foot leap, pulled off a Sleight of Hand 22 weight swap on the idol, then flew out on his Cloak of the Bat through a fireball.
Result: Got the idol. Got hit by a fireball but Dex saved for half. The single most "Mission Impossible" sequence of the entire session. Added a priceless idol to the party's loot haul.
Brilliant
#3
Opal
Didn't Learn the First Time
50%
2 Moments
▼
The Pressure Plate Leap
The situation: The party was carefully trying to figure out how to sneak around the gas trap pressure plates without triggering them.
What Opal did: Decided overthinking was for nerds and just leaped clean across the pressure plates.
Result: Cleared them completely. Saved the party time and bypassed the puzzle entirely. Sometimes Goliath legs are the answer. Proved that brute force can be an elegant solution.
Brilliant
The Mirror Push (Again)
The situation: The party reached the crystalline prism room. Everyone was analyzing the light beams and investigating the door. Opal had already watched Moor successfully use a mirror on the beach.
What Opal did: Tried to copy Moor's mirror trick from the beach. "I hate mirrors! Hold this mirror!" Grabbed a different mirror and shoved it -- despite having failed at this exact thing before.
Result: Rolled a Natural 1. The mirror reflected the thermal beam directly into her face. Took 14 damage to her eyes and went completely blind for the rest of the dungeon. Required a staff charge to fix her eyes. Cost the party a valuable resource.
Catastrophic
#4
Aramil
The Quiet Apocalypse
33%
3 Moments
▼
The Gorallon Whisperer
The situation: Beach ambush. Gorallons charging the party. Everyone preparing for combat.
What Aramil did: Cast Speak with Animals and tried to diplomatically convince the Gorallon to run away instead of fighting.
Result: It worked! The Gorallon fled. Avoided a full combat encounter and saved the party resources and time. Established that Aramil's first instinct is always diplomacy.
Brilliant
The Snake Whisperer
The situation: Giant Constrictor Snake just woke up. The party was gearing for a serious fight.
What Aramil did: Tried the same trick that worked on the beach -- Speak with Animals, called the snake "Brother," attempted diplomacy. Lightning doesn't strike twice.
Result: The snake was not interested in conversation. It grappled Aramil and squeezed him for 22 damage, dropping him to -10 HP. Nearly died. Taught the party that not every creature responds to diplomacy.
Catastrophic
The Eye Heist
The situation: The party was literally running to the boat to escape. Everyone was focused on getting out alive.
What Aramil did: While no one was looking, he quietly pried the golden eye from the snake idol and pocketed it. 5,000 gold. No one saw. No one was consulted.
Result: The sun went out. Eternal darkness fell over the entire continent of Faerun. The quiet ones are always the ones who break the world. Plunged the entire continent into eternal darkness.
Catastrophic
Lavender: 0 moments — Patient Planner ✓

Conspiracy Board

Session Conspiracy Board

9 Evidence Nodes -- 6 Active Connections -- 1 Debunked -- Lead Investigator: Aramil
CENTER PIECE -- THE CENTRAL MYSTERY
WHO ACTUALLY BENEFITS FROM THE GOLDEN SNAKE IDOL BEING REMOVED FROM THE TEMPLE
AND DID FIZZLEBLAST KNOW WHAT WOULD HAPPEN?
The idol was connected to the sun. Removing it plunged Faerun into eternal darkness. Fizzleblast hired expendable mercenaries to retrieve it. He stayed safely in his bubble. He didn't know what the artifact was -- or did he? And if Aramil hadn't stolen the eye, would the sun still have gone out anyway? **Why this is the center:** Every thread on this board radiates from this question. Fizzleblast's suspiciously vague instructions, the temple's sun-powered defense system, the murals warning of darkness, Aramil's secret theft, and the catastrophic outcome are all connected to the idol and whoever set this chain of events in motion. The stakes could not be higher -- the sun is gone. **Current best theory:** Fizzleblast knew more than he let on. He specifically wanted the idol but claimed not to know what it was. His "accidental" public posting attracted a rival party as a distraction. He stayed in a pre-built safety bubble while his hirelings did the dangerous work. Whether the eternal darkness was his goal or an unforeseen consequence is the question that breaks this case open.
Evidence Nodes (9)
E01Quote
CONFIRMED
"I don't know what it's called. I know it's golden and it's like snake-shaped... Touch nothing but the lamp. I mean, wrong franchise."
Source: Fizzleblast (DM), briefing on the shore
E04Quote
CONFIRMED
"If it gets me reinstated at the Royal Community College, then we're okay." -- Aramil, upon learning the idol may control the sun
Source: Aramil (Kyle 2), prism room discussion
E05Quote
CONFIRMED
"Not without the idol." -- Fizzleblast refuses entry to the safety bubble without the artifact
Source: Fizzleblast (DM), when party tries to enter the bubble
E10Event
CONFIRMED
Aramil secretly pried the Eye of the Serpent from the Golden Snake Idol with an 18 Sleight of Hand check. No other party member witnessed...
Show more
Aramil secretly pried the Eye of the Serpent from the Golden Snake Idol with an 18 Sleight of Hand check. No other party member witnessed it. Fizzleblast was asleep.
Source: The shore, during departure
E11Event
CONFIRMED
The sun went out. Eternal darkness fell on Faerun immediately after Aramil stole the eye. The DM stated: "The sun will not rise again in...
Show more
The sun went out. Eternal darkness fell on Faerun immediately after Aramil stole the eye. The DM stated: "The sun will not rise again in Faerun."
Source: Post-session revelation
E12Observation
CONFIRMED
Temple murals depicted two scenes: (1) A serpent coiled around a sun disk with rays bending toward its eye; (2) The serpent removed from...
Show more
Temple murals depicted two scenes: (1) A serpent coiled around a sun disk with rays bending toward its eye; (2) The serpent removed from the sun disk with the sun eclipsing and undead hordes rising from the ground.
Source: Antechamber murals, Aramil's history check
E13Observation
CONFIRMED
Aramil passed a history check and understood the murals implied that removing the idol would "throw the land into eternal darkness" -- and...
Show more
Aramil passed a history check and understood the murals implied that removing the idol would "throw the land into eternal darkness" -- and then proceeded to steal the eye anyway.
Source: Prism room, Aramil's history check (DC 4)
E14Observation
UNCERTAIN
Fizzleblast claimed not to know what the artifact was, only that it was "golden and snake-shaped." Yet he knew enough to hire a full...
Show more
Fizzleblast claimed not to know what the artifact was, only that it was "golden and snake-shaped." Yet he knew enough to hire a full expedition, rent canoes, build a safety field, and locate the exact temple. That is a suspicious amount of preparation for someone who "doesn't know what it is."
Source: Fizzleblast's briefing
E24Quote
CONFIRMED
"Vecna is laughing as this was his plan. You have set yourself up for the Tube of Annihilation campaign."
Source: DM (Blue Orbit), post-session revelation
Red String Connections
RED STRING: Strong / Confirmed YELLOW THREAD: Suspected / Circumstantial BLUE RIBBON: Theoretical / Inference GREEN X: Debunked
E01
E14Medium
Fizzleblast claims ignorance about the artifact but organized a precisely targeted expedition to retrieve it. His vagueness feels rehearsed, not genuine.
E10
E11High
Direct causation: Aramil steals the eye, the sun dies. The murals (E12) warned this would happen. This is the most certain connection on the board.
E12
E13High
Aramil read the murals, understood the warning about eternal darkness, and stole the eye anyway. Knowledge plus action equals culpability.
E04
E10High
Aramil's desire for academic reinstatement ("If it gets me reinstated at the Royal Community College") provides clear motive for the eye theft. Desperate men do desperate things.
E11
E24High
The DM explicitly connected the eternal darkness to Vecna's plan and a future campaign ("Tube of Annihilation"). The darkness is not an accident -- it is part of a larger cosmic scheme.
E05
E14Medium
Fizzleblast won't let the party into the bubble without the idol, yet claims not to know what the artifact is. He cares more about the artifact than the lives of his hirelings.
Debunked ConnectionsDISPROVEN
DEBUNKED
E13
E04Debunked
Originally theorized: DEBUNKED CONNECTION: Initial theory that Aramil didn't understand the murals and stole the eye ignorantly. He passed the history check. He understood. He chose to steal it anyway. The "ignorance defense" does not hold.
DEBUNKED CONNECTION: Initial theory that Aramil didn't understand the murals and stole the eye ignorantly. He passed the history check. He understood. He chose to steal it anyway. The "ignorance defense" does not hold.
Suspect List
Aramil
CONFIRMED CULPRIT (Eye Theft) / Unknown Alignment (Greater Conspiracy)
HIGH THREAT
8/10
Evidence For (4)
■Stole the Eye of the Serpent from the idol with a deliberate Sleight of Hand...
■Passed a history check on the murals and UNDERSTOOD that removing the idol/eye...
■Stated motive: "If it gets me reinstated at the Royal Community College, then...
Against (2)
■He seems genuinely motivated by petty academic ambition, not cosmic villainy....
■The DM attributed the darkness to Vecna's plan (E24), suggesting Aramil was an...
Full Evidence For
StrongStole the Eye of the Serpent from the idol with a deliberate Sleight of Hand check (18), hiding it from the entire party and Fizzleblast.
StrongPassed a history check on the murals and UNDERSTOOD that removing the idol/eye would cause eternal darkness -- then did it anyway.
StrongStated motive: "If it gets me reinstated at the Royal Community College, then we're okay." Academic desperation drove the theft.
ModerateHis warlock patron is unnamed and his pact details are murky. Who is pulling Aramil's strings? Could his patron benefit from eternal darkness?
Full Evidence Against
ModerateHe seems genuinely motivated by petty academic ambition, not cosmic villainy. He wanted to put the eye in his office, not end the world.
StrongThe DM attributed the darkness to Vecna's plan (E24), suggesting Aramil was an unwitting pawn, not a mastermind.
Key Question
Did Aramil's unnamed warlock patron direct him to steal the eye, or was it purely his own decision born from daddy issues and tenure anxiety?
Fizzleblast
Prime Suspect (Orchestrator)
MODERATE
6/10
Evidence For (5)
■Claimed not to know what the artifact was but organized a precisely targeted,...
■Built a pre-made magical safety bubble ("Fizzle Field, patent pending") before...
■"Accidentally" posted the expedition on public forums, attracting a rival party...
Against (3)
■He genuinely appeared neurotic, cowardly, and disorganized -- more "incompetent...
■He wanted the whole idol, not the eye. Aramil's theft was not part of any plan...
Full Evidence For
ModerateClaimed not to know what the artifact was but organized a precisely targeted, fully-equipped expedition to a specific temple in one of the most dangerous jungles in Faerun.
ModerateBuilt a pre-made magical safety bubble ("Fizzle Field, patent pending") before anyone entered the temple. He was prepared for things to go very wrong.
Weak"Accidentally" posted the expedition on public forums, attracting a rival party that served as a convenient distraction.
ModerateRefused to let the party into the safety bubble without the idol (E05). He cares more about the artifact than the lives of his hirelings.
WeakWas asleep when the party returned, completely unbothered by the undead apocalypse happening meters away.
Full Evidence Against
ModerateHe genuinely appeared neurotic, cowardly, and disorganized -- more "incompetent academic" than "secret villain."
StrongHe wanted the whole idol, not the eye. Aramil's theft was not part of any plan Fizzleblast set in motion.
ModerateHe has sent previous parties who all died. If he were orchestrating something, he is remarkably bad at it.
Key Question
What was Fizzleblast going to do with the idol once he had it? And what was the rest of his interrupted sentence: 'Whatever you do, do not press the--'?
Vecna
Mastermind (Identified by DM but unconfirmed in-world)
HIGH THREAT
9/10
Evidence For (3)
■The DM explicitly stated: "Vecna is laughing as this was his plan." Direct...
■Eternal darkness across Faerun is the kind of world-altering catastrophe that...
■The "Tube of Annihilation" campaign tease suggests this is a deliberate setup...
Against (2)
■The Vecna reference may have been a joke or DM flavor, not literal campaign...
■No in-world evidence of Vecna's involvement was presented during the session...
Full Evidence For
StrongThe DM explicitly stated: "Vecna is laughing as this was his plan." Direct attribution from the narrator.
StrongEternal darkness across Faerun is the kind of world-altering catastrophe that benefits a lich-god seeking to reshape reality.
StrongThe "Tube of Annihilation" campaign tease suggests this is a deliberate setup for a larger Vecna-driven plot.
Full Evidence Against
ModerateThe Vecna reference may have been a joke or DM flavor, not literal campaign canon. It came in the post-session wrap-up, not in-world.
ModerateNo in-world evidence of Vecna's involvement was presented during the session itself.
Key Question
Is the eternal darkness a permanent campaign consequence or a DM tease? If Vecna is real, did he manipulate Fizzleblast, Aramil, or both?
Unanswered Questions -- Ranked by Priority
CRITICAL
Can the eternal darkness be reversed by returning the Eye of the Serpent to the idol, or is the damage permanent?
This is the most important question in the world -- literally. If it can be reversed, there is a quest. If not,...
CRITICAL
What was Fizzleblast going to say? 'Whatever you do, do not press the--' WHAT?
Fizzleblast was interrupted mid-warning. The rest of that sentence could change everything.
HIGH
Does Aramil's warlock patron have a connection to the eternal darkness or to Vecna's plan?
Aramil's patron is never named. If his patron benefits from darkness, the theft may not have been Aramil's choice at...
HIGH
What does Fizzleblast actually want the idol for? Research? Sale? A ritual? Something worse?
He hired multiple expeditions with near-total fatality rates to get this one artifact. The motivation behind that...
THE CONSPIRACY THEORY
No, listen, LISTEN -- I've been staring at this board for six hours and it all connects. Fizzleblast is not an idiot. He's not some bumbling gnome who "accidentally" posted on a public forum and "doesn't know" what the artifact is. He's sent MULTIPLE parties into this temple. They ALL died. Every single one except one lunatic who grabbed an eagle. And what does Fizzleblast do? He keeps sending... Read full theory
No, listen, LISTEN -- I've been staring at this board for six hours and it all connects. Fizzleblast is not an idiot. He's not some bumbling gnome who "accidentally" posted on a public forum and "doesn't know" what the artifact is. He's sent MULTIPLE parties into this temple. They ALL died. Every single one except one lunatic who grabbed an eagle. And what does Fizzleblast do? He keeps sending more. Why? Because he NEEDS someone to remove that idol. He can't do it himself -- he's too weak, too cowardly, or bound by some restriction we don't understand yet. So he hires desperate people: a disgraced professor, a halfling who's "just here for the loot," a Goliath who literally can't see in the dark, a tiefling with a genie she owes freedom to, and a dragonborn cleric of justice. Expendable. Diverse enough that one of them will solve each puzzle. And the rival party? INSURANCE. If Charisma Company fails, Blackwater gets the idol and Fizzleblast negotiates for it after. He set up the collision deliberately. But here's the part that keeps me up: Vecna. The DM said Vecna is laughing. Vecna PLANNED this. What if Fizzleblast is Vecna's agent? What if the whole expedition was engineered so that someone -- ANYONE -- would remove the idol and trigger eternal darkness? And Aramil, poor, stupid, brilliant Aramil, didn't just take the bait -- he took the EXTRA bait. He stole the eye that nobody asked him to steal, and that was the final trigger. Vecna didn't need the idol removed. He needed the eye separated. And the one person in the party with the academic obsession, the daddy issues, the Gloves of Thievery, and the specific warlock abilities to pull it off... just HAPPENED to be on this expedition? No. No, no, no. Someone put Aramil on that team. Someone made sure he'd see those murals and understand them and steal the eye ANYWAY. The Tube of Annihilation isn't the sequel. It's the PLAN.
-- Aramil
Quote Book

Quote Book

18 Quotes Collected
Get fucked, Advil!
Aramil 🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥
Leaving the rival adventuring party (whose leader is presumably Indiana Jones-esque) to be eaten by zombies as Aramil flees.

If the men find out we can shapeshift, they're going to tell the church.

I hate mirrors! Hold this mirror!

Not cool, man. The sun will not rise again in Faerun.
