What HappenedHours passed. The sky remained black. No stars. No sun. The darkness that pressed against the Fizzle Field was not the darkness of night -- it was the darkness of forever.
ParticipantsAll (affected globally)
ConsequencesThe world has fundamentally changed. The session's final image: Aramil sitting apart, holding the warm gem, saying nothing.
Chronological Summary
LORE
#EventSessionLocationType
1Arrival, briefing, Loot ExplosionS1The ShoreTravel / Discovery
2Gorallon encounter -- mirror melt + intimidationS1Temple EntranceCombat / Social
3Antechamber pressure plates solvedS1Antechamber of BladesDiscovery
4Opal blinded by mirror beam (Nat 1)S1Prism RoomLoss
5Poison gas -- 14 damage to all; Gust of Wind dispersalS1Prism RoomCombat
6Giant Snake fight -- Aramil downed, Opal blind killS1Prism RoomCombat
7Temple puzzles completed -- portcullis opensS1Prism Room / Snake SanctumDiscovery
8Bob steals idol from above sleeping T-RexS1T-Rex CourtyardDiscovery / Turning Point
9Escape -- rival ambush, Phantasmal Force, blade trapsS1Entire Temple / ShoreCombat / Travel
10Aramil steals the Eye of the SerpentS1The ShoreTurning Point
11Dawn fails -- eternal darknessS1GlobalTurning Point / Loss
Event Chains
LORE
Chain: The Idol Retrieval

Fizzleblast hires party -> Temple entered -> Puzzles solved -> Idol stolen by Bob -> Idol delivered to Fizzleblast -> COMPLETED

Chain: The Eye Theft

Aramil investigates murals -> Learns serpent-sun connection -> Acquires Gloves of Thievery -> Steals the Eye (sleight of hand 18) -> Sun fails to rise -> Eternal darkness -> ACTIVE -- consequences unfolding, party unaware

Chain: The Rival Party

Fizzleblast's accidental public posting -> Rivals follow one hour behind -> Rivals observe from ridgeline -> Fireball ambush during extraction -> Phantasmal Force rout -> Rivals flee into jungle -> Undead rise -> Screams -> UNKNOWN -- fate undetermined

Unresolved Events
QUEST
EventSessionWhat's UnresolvedPotential Consequences
Aramil's theft of the Eye1No one knows. The world is in darkness because of one sleight of hand check.When discovered: party fracture, moral judgment from Moor, potential quest to return the Eye
Fizzleblast's interrupted warning1“Do not press the--” was cut off. What was he about to say?May relate to a trap or mechanism the party unknowingly triggered or avoided
Niles witnessed something1Found shaking on the floor of the bubble. What did he see through the translucent barrier?Potential testimony about what happened during the night -- undead, the darkness, or something worse
Rival party's fate1Fled into jungle before undead rose. Screams heard.Could return as undead, could have survived, could provide information about their unknown patron
The unknown buyer1Someone hired the rival party and has resources, knowledge, and intentA loose end -- they did not get what they paid for and may try again
At a Glance
QUEST
StatusCountQuests
Active1Undo the Eternal Darkness
Completed1Retrieve the Golden Snake Idol
Failed0--
Abandoned0--
Retrieve the Golden Snake Idol
QUEST
Quest Summary
FieldDetail
StatusCompleted
TypeMain Quest
Quest GiverFizzleblast
Completed InSession 1
ObjectiveRetrieve a golden, snake-shaped artifact from a Chultan jungle temple
OutcomeIdol retrieved by Bob and delivered to Fizzleblast. However, Aramil secretly removed one of the idol's eyes, causing catastrophic consequences.
Reward Received550 gp (credited, minus 770 gp in debits per Fizzleblast's invoice -- net: party owes 220 gp, forgiven in exchange for a five-star review). All temple loot retained by the party.
DurationSession 1 (single session)
Resolution Summary

The party entered the Temple of the Man and Crocodile, solved its pressure plate puzzles, survived its mirror traps and poison gas, defeated a Giant Constrictor Snake, navigated past a sleeping Tyrannosaur, and extracted the idol through Bob's climactic heist -- climbing the archway, stealing the idol, and flying out with the Cloak of the Bat. They escaped the temple under time pressure, routed the rival party's ambush, and delivered the artifact to Fizzleblast.

The quest was mechanically completed. The idol was retrieved and delivered. But the consequences of Aramil's secret theft of the Eye transformed a successful retrieval into an apocalyptic event.

Consequences & Ripple Effects
The Golden Snake Idol is in Fizzleblast's possession, but missing one eye
The Eye of the Serpent is secretly in Aramil's possession
The sun has not risen -- eternal darkness has fallen on Faerun
The undead are rising across the Chultan peninsula
Port Nyanzaru is in crisis
Fizzleblast has noticed the idol “feels lighter” and is asking “Where is the other eye?”
The rival party's unknown patron did not receive what they paid for
Undo the Eternal Darkness
QUEST
Quest Overview
FieldDetail
StatusActive (Implicit -- self-directed by consequences)
TypeMain Quest (World-Scale)
Quest GiverSelf-directed / Consequence of Aramil's actions
Given InSession 1 -- triggered by the theft of the Eye of the Serpent
ObjectiveReturn the Eye of the Serpent to the Golden Snake Idol and restore the sun
MotivationThe world is in darkness. The undead are rising. Civilization is collapsing.
RewardThe sun. Also, not dying in eternal darkness surrounded by undead.
DeadlineUnknown -- the longer the darkness persists, the more catastrophic the consequences
Difficulty AssessmentExtreme -- requires confronting Aramil's theft, returning to Chult, and placing the Eye back in the idol while navigating a now-darkened jungle full of risen undead and a very angry Tyrannosaur
Objective Breakdown & Progress
☐Discover that the Eye of the Serpent is missing from the idol
☐Identify Aramil as the thief
☐Confront Aramil and recover the Eye
☐Return to the Temple of the Man and Crocodile
☐Navigate the temple without solar-powered mechanisms (mirrors no longer function in darkness)
☐Return the Eye to the Golden Snake Idol
☐Confirm the sun rises
Complications & Obstacles
ComplicationDescriptionStatus
No one knows Aramil stole the EyeThe theft was performed in secret (sleight of hand 18)Active -- critical obstacle
The temple's mechanisms depend on sunlightWithout the sun, the mirrors, beams, and prism do not function -- the temple is a different place in darknessActive
The jungle is now full of undeadThe Chultan peninsula is experiencing a zombie apocalypseActive
Aramil may resist returning the EyeHis motivation (paternal approval, professional reinstatement) may override his guiltActive
The inscription warns “greed alone” cannot unbind what was takenReturning the Eye may require an act of selflessness or sacrificeUnknown
Notes

This quest exists as the natural continuation of the one-shot. If the campaign extends into the teased “Tube of Annihilation” sequel, this is the central throughline: undo what Aramil did. The challenge is not merely logistical (get the Eye back to the temple) but moral (confront the person who did this and decide what justice looks like). For Moor, as a cleric of Tyr, this is a test of everything his faith demands. For Opal, whose sister lives in Barovia -- a land that is now even darker -- this is personal. For Lavender, who values bonds above all else, Aramil's betrayal is a violation of the trust she gave freely. And for Bob, the pragmatist, the question is simpler: is Aramil still useful, and what is the fastest way to fix this?

Aramil's Reinstatement
LORE
FieldDetail
StatusActive (Aramil is still pursuing this)
Driven ByAramil
First ExpressedSession 1 -- “If it gets me reinstated at the Royal Community College, then we're okay”
ObjectiveRegain his position at the Royal Community College and earn his father's approval
ProgressStole the Eye of the Serpent, which he believes is valuable enough to warrant reinstatement
ObstaclesThe Eye may be cursed. The world is ending. His father may still not care.
ConnectionsDirectly conflicts with Undo the Eternal Darkness -- the Eye must be returned, but Aramil does not want to give it up
Opal's Rock
LORE
FieldDetail
StatusAbandoned (involuntarily)
Driven ByOpal
First ExpressedSession 1 -- “I go get it. I want it.”
ObjectiveRetrieve the black obsidian rock from the temple mechanism
ProgressIdentified the rock; could not retrieve it due to time pressure
ObstaclesThe rock is inside a temple in a jungle that is now dark and full of undead
ConnectionsCould serve as motivation for Opal to return to the temple (aligned with Undo the Eternal Darkness)
Lavender's Promise to Jezebel
LORE
FieldDetail
StatusActive (long-term)
Driven ByLavender
First ExpressedSession 1 -- “She made a deal with the genie that she would turn the genie free at the end”
ObjectiveFree Jezebel from the locket
ProgressNo progress during the session
ObstaclesThe conditions for freeing Jezebel are undefined
ConnectionsLavender's loyalty to Jezebel defines her character; this promise will test her when the cost becomes clear
Bob's Identity
LORE
FieldDetail
StatusUnknown
Driven ByBob (involuntarily -- his past pursues him)
First ExpressedSession 1 -- shifting facial features, “just Bob,” minimal backstory
ObjectiveUnknown -- Bob's goals beyond loot are deliberately obscured
ProgressN/A
ObstaclesBob himself -- he does not want this thread pulled
ConnectionsIf the campaign continues, Bob's identity will become relevant when the party needs trust and transparency
Immediate Priorities
LORE

1. Discover the connection between the missing Eye and the eternal darkness

2. Identify Aramil as the thief

3. Decide the party's response to Aramil's betrayal

4. Determine whether returning the Eye to the idol will restore the sun

Decisions Pending
LORE
Whether to confront Aramil or investigate independently
Whether to return to the temple or seek other solutions
Whether Fizzleblast is an ally or a liability in the restoration effort
How to navigate the Chultan jungle without sunlight
Leads Not Yet Followed
LORE
Fizzleblast has noticed the idol “feels lighter” -- he may independently discover the missing Eye
The temple inscription's warning about “greed alone” suggests a specific reversal condition
The Ubtao priest in Port Nyanzaru stated: “The serpent held the sun. Someone took the serpent's eye.”
The rival party's unknown patron remains at large and may have relevant knowledge

World Wiki current through Session 1 (One-shot). Compiled from the Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip session transcript.

THREAT
LOCATION
FACTION
ITEM
QUEST
LORE
SCROLL TO ZOOM · DRAG TO PAN · CLICK NODE TO VIEW
Backstories

Character Backstories

5 Characters / 35 Story Sections
Moor
“The Quiet Scales of Justice”
Muravurok
Cleric
Played by Kyle
CANONICAL FACTS
CONFIRMEDFull name is Muravurok; goes by Moor
CONFIRMEDDragonborn Cleric of Tyr, Life Domain
CONFIRMEDSpeaks in minimal, truncated sentences as a deliberate character choice
CONFIRMEDRecognized the symbol of Tyr above a door; instinctively grabbed the Scales of Justice (a Lawful Good item)
I
Origins & Early Life
▼

Muravurok is a Dragonborn raised in the service of Tyr. The specifics of his upbringing are not stated, but the confidence with which he carries his faith — and the instinctive, unsentimental way he heals — suggests someone who learned devotion through practice rather than preaching. The divine power he channels is not something he discovered; it reads as something he has always had.

II
Formative Events
▼

[Limited canonical data available for this period]

What can be inferred from Moor's behavior in the session is that his sense of duty was not learned recently. He heals without being asked, tests traps before letting others proceed, and speaks only when necessary. These are the habits of someone who has been responsible for other people's survival for a long time, and who learned that actions matter more than words.

III
Class Origin
▼

He did not choose to become a cleric. The word implies deliberation, and Muravurok did not deliberate. His devotion to Tyr is not mystical — it is structural. Tyr represents the principle that actions have consequences and that the strong bear responsibility for the weak. These are not abstract ideas to Muravurok; his behavior in the session demonstrates them as deeply held convictions.

When he recognized the symbol of Tyr above a door in a Chultan temple thousands of miles from his home, he did not comment on it. He simply noted it. The god's reach was long. The god's expectations were clear. Muravurok did not need to announce his faith. He carried it the way he carried his shield — visibly, functionally, without ornament.

IV
Key Relationships (Pre-Campaign)
▼

[Limited canonical data available for this period]

No pre-campaign relationships are mentioned. His behavior in the field suggests someone who has learned to care deeply about people he does not know well — the mark of a healer who has treated too many strangers to pretend that connection requires intimacy.

V
The Inciting Incident
▼

A flyer. A job posting. A gnome in cashmere offering gold for dangerous work in Chult. Muravurok answered it with the same lack of deliberation he brought to everything: the job needed a healer, and he was a healer. He did not pack a speech.

VI
The Deepest Motivation
▼

To be useful. Not celebrated, not remembered, not thanked — useful. Muravurok healed 148 points of damage in a single session without being asked to keep count. He tested traps with his own mace before letting anyone step forward. He tossed a Light-enchanted rock to enable someone else's heroic moment. His motivation is not glory. It is the bone-deep conviction that someone has to keep everyone alive, and that someone might as well be him.

VII
The Deepest Fear
▼

[Limited canonical data available for this period]

No fears were expressed in-session. His relentless focus on keeping others alive suggests a possible fear of failing those who depend on him, but this is speculative beyond what the session data supports.

CHARACTER VOICE
Speech patterns
Deliberately truncated. "Run through door. Why use many word when few word do trick?" Uses minimal vocabulary as a character philosophy, not a limitation. Kevin-from-The-Office cadence.
Decision-making style
Immediate and pragmatic. Tests dangers first, then acts decisively. Does not wait for consensus when the right course is clear.
Stress response
Gets even more clipped. Drops to single-word commands: "Go." "Leave." "Run." Does not panic; simply accelerates.
Relationship to others
Quiet caretaker. Monitors health constantly, heals without being asked, positions himself to protect others. Paternal without sentimentality.
Contradictions
Claims economy of action but expends enormous resources (spell slots, Channel Divinity, Staff charges) on others without hesitation. The most generous character in the party, operating behind a facade of blunt pragmatism.
Aramil
“The Adjunct Professor of Ruin”
Warlock
Played by Kyle
CANONICAL FACTS
CONFIRMEDHalf-Elf Warlock, archaeologist background
CONFIRMEDAdjunct Professor Emeritus at the Royal Community College
CONFIRMED"Sabbatical" from the college -- implied firing
CONFIRMEDDesperately seeks paternal approval: "Maybe my dad finally noticed me"
CONFIRMEDResembles "a very dingy, dirty Indiana Jones who has kind of lost it"
CONFIRMEDWears a purple crystal necklace and a stained fedora
CONFIRMEDStole the Eye of the Serpent; willing to sacrifice the world for personal validation
I
Origins & Early Life
▼
“Maybe my dad finally noticed me”

The boy grew up in the shadow of a man who was never there. Aramil is a half-elf, and his desperate need for paternal approval — "Maybe my dad finally noticed me" — indicates a father who was absent, dismissive, or both. The specifics of his childhood are unstated, but the wound is visible in everything he does: the insistence on titles, the hoarding of credentials, the willingness to sacrifice everything for recognition.

II
Formative Events
▼

Aramil held a position at the Royal Community College. His specialty was artifact recovery — the intersection of archaeology and arcane theory.

He was fired.

The details of his dismissal remain unclear — Aramil refers to it as a "sabbatical," a word he uses with the careful precision of someone who has practiced saying it in front of a mirror. What is known is that one day he was Adjunct Professor Aramil, and the next he was Adjunct Professor Emeritus — a title that sounds like a promotion and means exactly the opposite. Whether it was politics, misconduct, or institutional cruelty, the result was the same: the door closed, and Aramil stood on the outside with a stained fedora and a growing certainty that the world owed him something it was not willing to pay.

III
Class Origin
▼

Warlock magic is not earned through study or granted through faith — it is bargained for, and bargains are made by people who are desperate enough to accept terms they do not fully understand. The purple crystal that hangs against Aramil's chest is the visible remnant of whatever deal he struck, and he treats it the way a gambler treats a lucky coin: with the superstitious reverence of someone who knows that luck is the only thing between him and nothing.

The pact gave him power. Not the kind of power that impresses — not fireballs and flight — but the working tools of a man who lives by his wits: the ability to speak to beasts, to blast things from a distance, to conjure illusions convincing enough to make a professional soldier run. These were the powers of a con man, a negotiator, a survivor. They suited him.

IV
Key Relationships (Pre-Campaign)
▼
“Maybe my dad finally noticed me”

His father remains the defining absence. "Maybe my dad finally noticed me" — spoken casually, as if it were a joke, in the same tone a man uses when he is afraid that saying something sincerely will break him. The Eye of the Serpent, worth five thousand gold and possibly the world, was stolen not for wealth but for the fantasy that bringing home a prize large enough might finally make the old man look up from his papers and see his son.

V
The Inciting Incident
▼

Fizzleblast's posting was exactly the kind of opportunity Aramil had been waiting for: an artifact expedition led by someone too cowardly to enter the temple himself. A chance to recover something significant, something publishable, something that might force the community that rejected him to reconsider. He did not tell anyone his real reason for coming. He never does.

VI
The Deepest Motivation
▼

To be seen. Not by the world — Aramil has no interest in fame as such — but by the one person whose approval would fill the hollow space that has been growing inside him since childhood. Every artifact he acquires, every paper he writes, every title he insists upon is a letter addressed to a father who never opens the mail.

VII
The Deepest Fear
▼

[Limited canonical data available for this period]

The inversion of his deepest motivation is obvious: if his motivation is to be seen by his father, his fear is that his father has already seen him and looked away. But this was not stated in-session and remains speculative.

CHARACTER VOICE
Speech patterns
Academic and deflective. Uses titles and formal constructions to maintain distance. Answers direct questions with tangents or qualifications.
Decision-making style
Calculates risk-reward for himself. Makes unilateral decisions in secret. Does not consult, confess, or hesitate when personal gain is at stake.
Stress response
Becomes more desperate and less careful about concealing true motivations. Under maximum pressure, his mask slips entirely.
Relationship to others
Maintains a veneer of helpfulness that serves his own positioning. Competitive with peers (Lavender), dismissive of physical types (Opal), functional with allies who enable him (Bob, Moor).
Contradictions
Genuinely knowledgeable and intermittently helpful — his Speak with Animals saved the party, his Phantasmal Force enabled the escape — but every contribution is ultimately in service of personal advancement. The most capable and the most selfish member of the party.
Opal
“The Stone That Feels”
Barbarian
Played by Shannon
CANONICAL FACTS
CONFIRMEDGoliath Barbarian, physically massive, "hewed and jacked"
CONFIRMEDSelf-described as "kind of dumb"
CONFIRMEDSister named Pearl living happily in Barovia
CONFIRMEDSaw a flyer for Fizzleblast's expedition
CONFIRMEDEmotional range described as "calm or angry"
I
Origins & Early Life
▼
“hewed and jacked,”

Opal is a Goliath, "hewed and jacked," and self-describes as "kind of dumb." The specifics of her upbringing are unstated, but her physical confidence and direct approach to every problem suggest she was raised in a community that valued strength and endurance above intellectual pursuits. She learned to hit things very hard before she learned that hitting things was not always the answer. She still has not entirely absorbed that last lesson.

II
Formative Events
▼
“just hanging out, living a very happy life, actually.”

Opal has a sister named Pearl who now lives in Barovia — "just hanging out, living a very happy life, actually." The way Opal says it, with the careful brightness of someone repeating a fact they have rehearsed, suggests that the separation was not easy, even if it was right. Pearl is safe. Pearl is happy. Opal does not need to protect her anymore. And the absence of that purpose left a space that Opal has been trying to fill with adventure, obsidian rocks, and the sheer delight of hitting things ever since.

III
Class Origin
▼

Rage, for Opal, is not anger. It is clarity. When the fury takes her, the world simplifies to its essential components: the target, the axe, the arc between them. The fog of confusion that clouds her thinking in ordinary life — the math she cannot do, the words she cannot parse, the social nuances that slide past her like water over stone — all of it burns away, and what remains is pure, brilliant, physical intelligence. Her body knows things her mind does not. When she killed the Giant Constrictor Snake while blind, she did not need to see it. She heard the scales, felt the vibration through the stone, and let her muscles remember what her eyes could not.

IV
Key Relationships (Pre-Campaign)
▼

Pearl is the anchor. Everything Opal does — the cheerfulness, the fearlessness, the willingness to take damage so others do not — traces back to the sister she spent her childhood protecting. The habit of care is so deeply embedded that it extends to strangers: she held Lavender's hand when she was blind, not because she needed comfort, but because physical contact is how Opal communicates love, and she decided in that moment that Lavender deserved it.

V
The Inciting Incident
▼

She saw a flyer. That is the beginning and the end of the deliberation. Opal does not weigh options. She sees something that interests her, and she goes toward it with the full momentum of a Goliath who has never learned to hesitate. Chult had dinosaurs. Opal likes dinosaurs. The rest is logistics.

VI
The Deepest Motivation
▼

To find things worth caring about. Pearl is safe and does not need her. The mountain is behind her. The world is full of obsidian rocks and dinosaurs and people who are smaller than her and need protecting. Opal is not searching for meaning in any philosophical sense — she is searching for the next thing to hold, the next person to stand in front of, the next fight that will make her feel the way she felt when Pearl looked up at her with trust.

VII
The Deepest Fear
▼
“I didn't get my rock”

"I didn't get my rock" — said at the end of the session with the quiet sadness of someone who knows that the things she values are not the things the world values. That single line reveals more than any invented backstory could: Opal's fears are small and concrete, not grand or existential, and that makes them all the more affecting.

CHARACTER VOICE
Speech patterns
Simple, direct, enthusiastic. Short declarative sentences. Occasional confusion about game mechanics mirrored in character as confusion about complex situations.
Decision-making style
Immediate and physical. If it can be chopped, pushed, or punched, she is first in line. Defers to others for anything requiring patience or reading.
Stress response
Gets louder and more physically aggressive. "I hate mirrors! Hold this mirror!" — anger at the obstacle, then direct engagement.
Relationship to others
Warm, protective, physically affectionate. Forms attachments quickly and does not let go easily.
Contradictions
Describes herself as feeling only "calm or angry," but demonstrates tenderness (holding Lavender's hand), attachment (the obsidian rock), and sadness (leaving things behind) that suggest a richer emotional landscape than she admits.
Lavender
“The Girl Who Found a Lamp”
Warlock
Played by Kelsey
CANONICAL FACTS
CONFIRMEDTiefling Warlock, Genie Patron (Pact of the Chain)
CONFIRMEDDid not grow up magical; became a Warlock recently
CONFIRMEDFound a locket containing a genie named Jezebel
CONFIRMEDMade a deal with Jezebel: warlock powers in exchange for eventually freeing the genie
CONFIRMEDHas an imp familiar named Tika with a New Jersey accent
I
Origins & Early Life
▼

Lavender grew up ordinary, which for a Tiefling means growing up visible. She responded to this by making herself worth looking at. Jewels on the horns. Bright clothing. What is known is that before the locket, Lavender was not magical. She was a Tiefling without the sorcery that bloodline sometimes carries, a woman defined by her appearance rather than her abilities.

II
Formative Events
▼

She found a locket containing a genie named Jezebel. The circumstances of how she found it are not described. Jezebel offered warlock powers — real magic — in exchange for a promise: free me, eventually, when you can.

Lavender said yes. Not because she was desperate, not because she was greedy (she is demonstrably not), but because someone was trapped and asking for help, and Lavender is constitutionally incapable of hearing that request and walking away.

III
Class Origin
▼
“I don't know how to play D&D”

She did not train for warlock magic. She did not study for it. One day she was a Tiefling with jeweled horns and no particular skills, and the next she was pointing her fingers at things and watching them explode in beams of force. "I don't know how to play D&D" — said at the table, but it maps perfectly onto the character: Lavender is learning as she goes, discovering what she can do in the moments she needs to do it.

Tika appeared shortly after the pact. An imp with a New Jersey accent and the weary professionalism of a familiar who has seen better postings. He shapeshifts between raven and rat and complains about both. He is, in many ways, the partner Lavender did not know she needed — a second set of eyes, a scout, a sarcastic conscience that follows her into danger and occasionally beats her there.

IV
Key Relationships (Pre-Campaign)
▼
“If she says do it, I'm going to do it. She's my butt.”

Jezebel is the relationship that defines her. A bond built on a promise — power in exchange for freedom — that Lavender treats not as a transaction but as a friendship. "If she says do it, I'm going to do it. She's my butt." The syntax is clumsy. The loyalty is not. Lavender does what Jezebel asks because she trusts the genie, and trust, for Lavender, is the foundation of everything.

Tika is the relationship that sustains her. When the imp died in the snake's jaws, Lavender spent ten minutes — a long time, in a temple that was actively trying to kill them — to bring him back. Not because she needed his abilities (though she did). Because he was her friend, and she does not leave friends behind.

V
The Inciting Incident
▼

Why she answered Fizzleblast's call is not stated. What is clear is that Lavender arrived in Chult with optimism that seemed medically implausible given the circumstances, and maintained it through poison gas, blinding beams, giant snakes, and a fireball. She is either brave or oblivious, and the distinction may not matter.

VI
The Deepest Motivation
▼

To prove that kindness is not weakness. Lavender gave away items she found. She held a blind woman's hand. She offered to sacrifice her most powerful tool to stop the heat. She was named MVP not through aggression but through care — using every ability she had to prevent harm, enable others, and keep the group together. In a world that often rewards selfishness (see: Aramil), Lavender is the argument that generosity is its own kind of power.

VII
The Deepest Fear
▼

That the deal she made will cost more than she can pay. Freeing Jezebel is a promise she made sincerely, and Lavender does not break promises. But the terms of that freedom are unknown, and the gap between good intentions and what the universe actually demands is where tragedies live.

CHARACTER VOICE
Speech patterns
Casual, modern, with pop culture references delivered deadpan. Thinks out loud about mechanics and options. Quick reactions, often finishing others' sentences.
Decision-making style
Weighs group needs against her resources. Saves her best tools for the worst moments. Generous by default, strategic by necessity.
Stress response
More focused and decisive. "We need to leave." Cuts through debate with clarity.
Relationship to others
Nurturing without being maternal. Sarcastic but never cruel. Forms bonds quickly and fights to maintain them.
Contradictions
Claims not to know what she is doing ("I don't know how to play D&D") while consistently making the best decisions in the party. The gap between self-assessment and actual competence is vast.
Bob
“The Night”
Rogue
Played by Josh
CANONICAL FACTS
CONFIRMEDHalfling Rogue (Thief archetype), name is just "Bob"
RUMOREDClaims to be married
CONFIRMEDFacial features may shift -- nose and mustache "may or may not have changed"
CONFIRMED"I'm just here for the loot, baby"
I
Origins & Early Life
▼

Nobody knows where Bob came from, and Bob is not talking. This is not evasion born of trauma or shame. It is a professional boundary. Bob's past is behind him, and behind him is a place he does not invite visitors.

II
Formative Events
▼

The shifting features suggest something specific: either a magical disguise, a racial trait he has not disclosed, or a past that required him to become someone new more than once. A Halfling whose face changes is a Halfling who has needed to stop being recognizable, and people who need to stop being recognizable usually have a story that involves running.

Bob is not merely good at what he does — he is the kind of good that comes from repetition so extensive that the skill has moved from conscious thought to reflex. His stealth and sleight of hand are not talent; they are survival encoded in muscle memory.

III
Class Origin
▼

Bob does not think of himself as a Rogue. He thinks of himself as a professional. The distinction matters: a rogue steals because they can. A professional acquires because the job requires it. Bob's relationship with loot is not greed — it is craft. He evaluates items the way a jeweler evaluates stones: dispassionately, accurately, and with an eye toward who could use them best. He found the Belt of Hill Giant Strength and gave it to Opal. He found the Cloak of the Bat and kept it. Both decisions were correct.

IV
Key Relationships (Pre-Campaign)
▼
“I'm, uh... Married.”

"I'm, uh... Married." Delivered with the kind of pause that could mean "I am thinking about how to phrase this truth" or "I am inventing a fact to avoid providing a real one." The claim is listed as RUMORED in his canonical anchors for good reason.

V
The Inciting Incident
▼

Loot. Bob answered Fizzleblast's call because there was treasure in a temple and someone willing to pay for its retrieval. He did not need a deeper reason. But the fact that he stayed — that he screamed down corridors to draw enemies away from people he had met hours ago, that he hung by his legs over a tyrannosaur for a party objective — suggests that somewhere in the professional facade, there is a person who has decided that these particular strangers are worth the risk.

VI
The Deepest Motivation
▼

Bob's persona — the hood, the mystery, the loot-first attitude — is a controlled narrative. He decides what people see. He decides what people know. In a world where his face literally shifts, this control is not vanity but necessity: if he cannot control what he looks like, he will control what he means. "Just Bob" is not a name. It is a statement of intent: I am exactly this, nothing more, and you do not get to look deeper.

VII
The Deepest Fear
▼

[Limited canonical data available for this period]

Bob did not express fears in-session. His deliberate obscurity and shifting features suggest someone who values anonymity above all else, but what he is afraid of losing — or being found out for — remains unknown.

CHARACTER VOICE
Speech patterns
Short, sarcastic, modern. Quips and one-liners. Does not dominate conversations but delivers the best punchlines. "And how many squirtons in a gusher?"
Decision-making style
Cost-benefit analysis weighted toward loot and survival. Calculated risk-taking disguised as chaos. Volunteers for the hardest jobs because he knows he can execute them.
Stress response
Gets quieter and more focused. The jokes stop. The skills sharpen. During the idol heist, he did not crack a single joke.
Relationship to others
Maintains distance through humor and mystery. Generous with loot acquisitions for others despite his stated mercenary ethos.
Contradictions
Says he is only there for the loot. Screams down corridors to save the party. Gives away the best items he finds. The gap between the persona and the person is the character.
In-World Documents

In-World Documents

6 Artifacts -- Letters, Scrolls & Sacred Inscriptions
Document 1:Fizzleblast's Expedition Contract
Elegant but small script, clearly written by someone with formal training but limited patience
CONTRACT
▼
A single sheet of moderately expensive parchment, folded twice into quarters and sealed with a glob of blue wax impressed with a stylized explosion -- the Charisma Company seal.

Elegant but small script, clearly written by someone with formal training but limited patience

EXPEDITION CONTRACT Charisma Company -- Chultan Artifact Retrieval Division Proprietor: Fizzleblast, Wiz., M.Arc., F.R.S. (Pending)

Blue wax seal, partially obscured by a thumbprint -- the seal depicts a starburst with the letters "CC" in the center

This contract, executed on the date left blank -- the author forgot to fill it in day of month also blank, in the sovereign port of Nyanzaru, hereby establishes the terms of engagement between CHARISMA COMPANY (hereafter "the Company," "the Employer," "Fizzleblast," or "Me") and the UNDERSIGNED ADVENTURER (hereafter "the Contractor," "the Hired Muscle," or "You").

ARTICLE I: SCOPE OF WORK

The Contractor agrees to enter a temple of indeterminate age and the word "moderate" is crossed out, replaced with "manageable," then crossed out again and replaced with "non-fatal" danger located in the Chultan interior, approximately a number has been erased and rewritten three times -- the final figure reads "2" hours upriver from the landing site designated "Fizzle Field."

The Contractor shall retrieve ONE (1) artifact described as GOLDEN and SNAKE-SHAPED from the temple's interior vaults. Margin note in smaller script: "Touch nothing but the lamp -- WRONG FRANCHISE, delete this before printing" This note was not deleted before printing.

All other treasures, relics, weapons, armor, curiosities, and the word "garbage" is crossed out incidental finds within the temple are the property of the Contractor upon removal from the premises.

ARTICLE II: COMPENSATION

The Contractor shall receive: - Retention of all non-specified loot found within the temple - Access to Charisma Company's patented Fizzle Field safety perimeter for the duration of the expedition - Use of one (1) canoe margin note: "RENTAL -- DO NOT DAMAGE -- I will dock your pay for scratches" - A signing bonus of magical equipment to be distributed at the Employer's discretion a second margin note in different ink: "Niles dropped the bag. Consider the signing bonus whatever you caught."

The Contractor shall NOT receive: - Hazard pay - Medical coverage - Burial services - Emotional support - The golden snake artifact (THIS IS MINE)

ARTICLE III: TIMELINE

The expedition shall commence at DAWN and conclude no later than the following NOON.

Underlined three times: The canoe rental expires at noon. NOT 12:01. NOON. I am not paying the late fee again.

ARTICLE IV: LIABILITY

The Employer accepts no liability for injury, death, dismemberment, petrification, polymorph this word is circled with an exclamation mark, madness, cursing, or existential dread sustained during the expedition. The Contractor acknowledges that the temple may contain but is not limited to: bladed traps, thermal arrays, venomous creatures, structural instability, and long pause in the writing, as if the author considered and rejected several words surprises.

Different ink, added later: The Employer also accepts no liability for rival adventuring parties that may or may not have been alerted to the expedition's existence through an accidental posting on a public forum. This was not the Employer's fault. The forum interface is the word "confusing" is crossed out poorly designed.

ARTICLE V: TERMINATION

This contract terminates upon: a) Successful delivery of the golden snake artifact to the Employer b) Death of the Contractor margin note: "preferably not" c) crossed out: "Mutual agreement" The Employer saying so

ARTICLE VI: NON-COMPETE

The Contractor agrees not to sell, trade, barter, or gift the golden snake artifact to any rival organization, competing adventuring company, or ink blot mothers-in-law.

SIGNED:

An elaborate signature reading "Fizzleblast" with no fewer than seven unnecessary flourishes. The "F" is twice the height of the other letters.

CONTRACTOR:

Five blank lines. Only two have marks on them: Line 1: A single word in block letters: "MOOR" Line 3: A scrawl that might say "Bob" or might be a small drawing of a circle

At the very bottom of the page, in tiny script: Charisma Company is not responsible for the end of the world, the dimming of the sun, or any eschatological events that may or may not result from artifact removal. All complaints should be directed to the Employer's intern.

Document 2:Temple Entrance Inscription -- The Man and the Crocodile
Carved in Old Chultan glyphs; translated below
INSCRIPTION
▼
Deeply carved glyphs in a stone lintel, weathered but still legible.

Carved in Old Chultan glyphs; translated below

The first line is carved deeper than the rest, the characters nearly an inch into the stone:

TWO WHO WALK AS ONE SHALL PASS UNHARMED.

Below, in slightly smaller characters:

The Man steps with the Crocodile. The Crocodile steps with the Man. Neither leads. Neither follows. Their blades cross overhead -- not in combat, but in covenant.

A horizontal line separates this from the next section. Below the line, the carving style changes -- less formal, more hurried, as though added later by a different hand:

When the Serpent's Eye looks upon the world, the Sun answers. When the Eye is taken, the Sun forgets.

At the bottom, nearly eroded to illegibility, a final line in the oldest carving style:

Several glyphs are damaged beyond recognition ... WHAT IS BOUND ... damaged ... SHALL NOT BE UNBOUND BY ... damaged ... GREED ALONE.

Margin carving, scratched rather than chiseled -- clearly vandalism from a later period, in Common script: "Blacktooth's Blades were here -- Year of the Scarlet Moon"

Below the vandalism, in different but equally crude scratching: "Blacktooth's Blades were NOT here the following year -- Ed."

Document 3:Aramil's Research Notes -- The Serpent's Eye
Page 1 -- written in hurried academic shorthand
NOTES
▼
Three pages torn from a cheap notebook, folded twice and jammed inside the crown of a stained fedora.

Page 1 -- written in hurried academic shorthand

FIELD NOTES -- Chultan Temple (unnamed), Interior Survey Expedition: Charisma Co. (Fizzleblast, patron -- unreliable) Date: blank -- Aramil did not bother recording the date

Antechamber mural -- Man + Crocodile motif. Paired pressure plates confirm: the temple's security is built on COOPERATION. Two must walk as one. Whoever built this understood that the greatest threats come from individuals acting alone.

margin note: Ironic.

Heliographic arrays -- pre-Netherese? Solar engineering of this sophistication suggests a civilization with advanced understanding of optics and thermodynamics. The mirrors are not magical -- they are MECHANICAL. The magic is in the prism and the gems. The mirrors simply focus sunlight. Note: Could the same principle be used to this thought trails off without completion

Page 2 -- writing becomes more agitated

Snake statues = reflectors. The prism splits the beam. Four gems above the portcullis need to be lit simultaneously. The ENTIRE TEMPLE is a solar clock -- the beams only align at specific times of day. After sunset, the mechanism goes dark. Everything that depends on sunlight -- the traps, the mirrors, the gems --

large underline: EVERYTHING DEPENDS ON SUNLIGHT.

The serpent idol. Gold. Snake-coiled around a sun disk. Two eyes -- gemstones, possible chrysoberyl or alexandrite variant. The inscription says the Serpent's Eye "looks upon the world" and "the Sun answers."

Hypothesis: the gems are not decorative. They are FUNCTIONAL. The idol is the temple's anchor -- the thing that binds the solar mechanism to whatever divine or arcane principle sustains it.

different ink, added later: I was right. I was right and I took it anyway.

Page 3 -- handwriting deteriorates significantly

The Eye.

crossed out: I shouldn't have crossed out: Father would say crossed out: It's not too late to

Sleight of hand, 18. Nobody saw. The gem is warm. Warmer than body temperature. Warmer than the jungle. It feels like holding a coal that does not burn.

Est. value: 5,000 gold. Enough for crossed out: "reinstatement" crossed out: "a new start" the final attempt reads: enough.

At the bottom of the page, in very small writing, pressed hard enough to tear the paper slightly:

The sun should have risen by now.

The circular burn mark from the Eye sits directly below this line. The paper is slightly translucent at the mark, as though the heat changed its molecular structure.

On the reverse of page 3, a single line in shaking handwriting:

It was supposed to be enough.

Document 4:Blackwater's Orders -- Rival Party Briefing
Disguised block lettering on dark parchment
LETTER
▼
A single sheet of dark parchment, folded into thirds and sealed with black wax bearing no insignia -- deliberately anonymous.

Disguised block lettering on dark parchment

TO: TEAM LEADER, BLACKWATER RE: CHULTAN RETRIEVAL -- PRIORITY TARGET

Your window opens tomorrow at dawn. The gnome's expedition launches at first light from the southern landing. He has hired five -- descriptions attached no attachment found -- likely separated in transit or the jungle.

OBJECTIVE: Acquire the golden serpent artifact from the temple interior. The gnome has identified the correct temple. Let his team do the work. You follow.

METHOD: Do NOT enter the temple ahead of them. The heliographic arrays will kill you. Underlined: I have lost people to those mirrors before. Wait until the gnome's team has cleared the interior mechanisms, then move. If they solve the light puzzles, the beams will be redirected away from the corridors. Their success creates your path.

TIMING: You are one hour behind them. This is deliberate. Maintain the gap. If you arrive too early, you compete with them in the traps. If you arrive too late, they escape with the artifact. One hour. Margin note in same hand: Bring the dimension door scrolls. You will need a fast extraction.

CONTINGENCY: If the gnome's team reaches the artifact first, you have two options: 1. Ambush during their exit. The temple has one entrance. They must come out the way they went in. Set positions at the canyon mouth. 2. If ambush fails, the gnome's safety field cannot be breached by physical force. Wait for them to break camp and take them on the water.

RULES OF ENGAGEMENT: - Lethal force authorized. This line was written, crossed out, then written again. - Minimize magical expenditure until the extraction. You will need spell slots for the return journey. - The artifact is worth more than your team. Act accordingly.

At the bottom, in slightly different pressure, as though added as an afterthought:

The gnome does not know what the artifact does. Neither do I. But the buyer is paying enough that I do not care. If it does something when you take it from the temple, deal with the consequences. That is what I am paying you for.

No signature. No name. The wax seal is blank.

On the reverse, in a different hand -- hastier, written with charcoal rather than ink:

the gnome posted on PUBLIC forums -- he's either stupid or wants us here the half-elf in the fedora keeps looking at the gem eyes on the idol -- watch him the dragonborn is the healer. kill him first if it comes to a fight

A crude sketch of the temple entrance, drawn from memory, with the mirror positions marked in charcoal. One mirror is circled with the note: "break this one to kill the beams"

Document 5:Fizzleblast's Post-Expedition Invoice
Precise script on high-quality linen paper
INVOICE
▼
A crisp sheet of high-quality linen paper -- Fizzleblast clearly brought his stationery kit on the expedition.

Precise script on high-quality linen paper

CHARISMA COMPANY Artifact Retrieval -- Expeditionary Services -- Cashmere Consultation

Blue wax seal with CC starburst

POST-EXPEDITION FINANCIAL RECONCILIATION Project: Chultan Temple Retrieval (CODENAME: Fieldtrip)

TO: The Contractors (collectively, "You Lot") FROM: Fizzleblast, Wiz., M.Arc., F.R.S. (Still Pending)

---

CREDITS (Owed to Contractor)

| Item | Amount | |------|--------| | Artifact retrieval fee (golden snake idol, delivered intact) | 500 gp | | Hazard bonus (discretionary -- you all looked very beaten up) | 0 gp | | Performance bonus (nobody died -- unprecedented) | 50 gp | | Subtotal | 550 gp |

DEBITS (Owed to Charisma Company)

| Item | Amount | |------|--------| | Magical equipment distribution (signing bonus, via Niles) | 350 gp | | Potion of Instant Attunement (shattered -- NILES) | 200 gp | | Canoe rental (2x canoe, 24-hour lease, Port Nyanzaru Marina) | 25 gp | | Canoe damage surcharge (scratches on hull, stern section) | 10 gp | | Fizzle Field deployment and maintenance (12 hours) | 150 gp | | Niles (labor, emotional damages, therapy estimate) | 5 gp | | Cashmere cleaning (salt spray, jungle particulate, one unidentified stain) | 30 gp | | crossed out: "Emotional distress suffered by the Employer watching through the bubble" | crossed out: "75 gp" |

| Subtotal | 770 gp |

---

NET BALANCE: (220 gp) owed TO Charisma Company

In red ink, underlined: You owe ME money.

Below, in smaller script: However, given that you successfully retrieved the artifact and all survived (truly unprecedented -- my last three teams did not), I am prepared to forgive the balance in exchange for the artifact and a positive review on the Mercenaries, Murders, and Mothers-in-Law forum. Five stars. Minimum.

At the very bottom, in green ink, clearly added later -- the handwriting is shaking:

ADDENDUM: The sun has not risen. I have checked my calculations, my calendar, and my pocket watch. It is now four hours past dawn and the sky is black. This is... several words scratched out I am certain this is unrelated to the expedition. Almost certain. Mostly.

Margin note in the same green ink: Why does the idol feel lighter?

Final margin note, barely legible: Where is the other eye?

Document 6:The Darkness Broadsheet -- Port Nyanzaru Herald
Woodblock-printed broadsheet, uneven ink distribution
BROADSHEET
▼
A single broadsheet, cheaply printed on rough pulp paper using a basic woodblock press.

Woodblock-printed broadsheet, uneven ink distribution

THE PORT NYANZARU HERALD "All the News That Survives the Jungle" Crude woodcut of Port Nyanzaru's harbor, clearly reused from a previous edition

---

## THE SUN HAS NOT RISEN

Merchants Guild Calls Emergency Session -- Dinosaur Races Cancelled -- Temples Overwhelmed

The sun did not rise this morning.

This reporter wishes to add additional context, explanation, or reassurance to that statement, but there is none to add. The sun did not rise. It is now the time is left blank -- the printer apparently could not keep up with the passage of hours past the expected dawn, and the sky over Port Nyanzaru -- over all of Chult, as far as can be determined -- remains the same empty black it has been since midnight.

The Merchant Princes have convened an emergency session in the Grand Souk. Kwayothe, whose control of the city's oil and candle supply has made her the most powerful person in Nyanzaru overnight, has reportedly tripled prices already. Margin stamp in red ink from the Herald's editor: "CONFIRMED -- candles now 3 sp each. Torches 5 sp. Lantern oil 1 gp per flask."

The temples of Savras, Waukeen, and Tyr a small symbol of scales appears here, hand-stamped have opened their doors to the frightened. The temple of Ubtao -- or what remains of its congregation -- has been silent. One priest, who declined to give his name, was heard saying: "The Old One warned us. The serpent held the sun. Someone took the serpent's eye."

A crude woodcut illustration, clearly carved in haste, depicts a snake coiled around a circle representing the sun. One of the snake's eyes is blank.

No official explanation has been provided by the Merchant Princes. The harbormaster has suspended all outgoing vessels pending "improved visibility," a phrase that this reporter suspects may prove optimistic.

UNDEAD SIGHTINGS REPORTED ACROSS THE PENINSULA

Multiple reports have reached the Herald of corpses rising from graves, burial sites, and jungle floors across the Chultan peninsula. A merchant caravan arriving the word "this morning" is crossed out and replaced with "during the current period of darkness" from Camp Vengeance reported that "the dead walked out of the trees and simply stood there, as if waiting for something."

The Flaming Fist garrison at Fort Beluarian has not responded to communication attempts. A runner dispatched at time blank has not returned.

City guard patrols have been doubled. Citizens are advised to remain indoors, keep lights burning, and avoid the harbor district after the concept of "after dark" has been crossed out, since it is always dark now at all times.

WHAT WE KNOW: - The sun did not rise at its expected time - The stars are not visible - The undead are rising across the peninsula - Candle and oil prices have increased dramatically - The temples are open but have no answers

WHAT WE DO NOT KNOW: - Why - For how long - Whether the sun will return - This line has been left blank, as if the author considered writing something and decided it was too frightening to print

---

At the bottom of the broadsheet, in the advertising section, printed before the current crisis:

CHARISMA COMPANY -- EXPEDITIONARY SERVICES "We Go Where You Won't!" Now hiring: experienced adventurers for artifact retrieval. Competitive compensation. Equipment provided. Survival not guaranteed. Contact: Fizzleblast, c/o Port Nyanzaru Marina, Slip 7.

Someone has drawn a crude arrow from this advertisement to the headline about the sun, and written in charcoal: "COINCIDENCE?"

Below that, in different charcoal: "Definitely not."

Novelization

Campaign Novelization

5-Chapter Prose Retelling

"Gold is heavy, but history is heavier still. And in Chult, the history is hungry."

5 CHAPTERS
+The Cashmere and the Clay

The air in Chult did not merely exist; it pressed. It was a physical weight, a wet wool blanket draped over the lungs, smelling of rotting vegetation, wet fur, and the ancient, reptilian musk of things that had not evolved in ten million years.

Moor wiped a hand across his snout, his scales slick with condensation. The cleric of Tyr stood knee-deep in the surf, the sand sucking at his sabatons, watching their employer fuss over the hem of a robe that cost more than Moor’s entire monastery.

"The cashmere, Niles! The cashmere!" Fizzleblast shrieked, his voice cracking like a dry twig. The gnome was a caricature of an academic—beard too big, spectacles too thick, courage too small. "If it touches the water, it shrivels. Do you want me to shrivel, Niles?"

Niles, a red-headed boy whose limbs seemed to have grown faster than his coordination, threw himself into the mud to serve as a human bridge. Fizzleblast stepped delicately onto the boy’s back, hopping to the dry sand without dampening a single thread.

Moor sighed, a low rumble in his chest. "We are walking into a tomb," he muttered, though no one was listening.

The party—if one could call this loose association of mercenaries and desperate souls a party—was already scrambling up the beach. There was Opal, a Goliath woman who looked as though she had been hewn from granite, currently checking the edge of a greataxe that was larger than the gnome hiring them. There was Lavender, a Tiefling whose horns were bedecked in jewels that caught the harsh jungle sun, radiating a manic optimism that Moor found deeply unsettling. Aramil, the half-elf archaeologist, looked like a man continuously on the verge of a nervous breakdown, clutching a purple crystal and muttering about tenure. And somewhere, supposedly, was Bob. The halfling was a shadow in a hood, a collection of knives and pockets that occasionally manifested a nose or a mustache.

"Coordinates posted publicly," Fizzleblast was saying, talking fast to outrun the jungle silence. "Administrative error. Mercenaries, Murderers, and Mothers-in-Law forum. Dreadful mistake. The Red Fangs are an hour behind us."

"An hour?" Aramil’s voice pitched up. He looked back at the tree line as if expecting an arrow.

"Give or take. They’ll kill you, of course. Ransom me. But look!" Fizzleblast gestured grandly toward the tree line.

Ahead, the jungle broke against a wall of stone. The temple rose in tiered steps, a ziggurat of moss-slicked grey rock. But it was the light that drew the eye. Mirrors—dozens of them, polished to a killing shine—were arrayed along the terraces, catching the midday sun and focusing it into blinding, searing beams that crisscrossed the entrance.

"Heliographic thermal arrays," Fizzleblast said, jamming a metal rod into the sand. A shimmer of blue force sprang up—a magical dome. "I shall supervise from here. The Fizzle Field. Repels organic matter and aggressive sales tactics. You get the artifact—golden, snake-shaped, I don't know, I haven't seen it—and you keep the rest. Just get back before sunset. Or before the Red Fangs kill you. Or the dinosaurs."

"Dinosaurs?" Opal asked, squinting at the jungle.

"Smell that?" Moor asked.

Opal sniffed. "Scales. Big ones."

"Go," Fizzleblast shooed them. "Niles! The loot sack!"

The boy stumbled forward, tripping over a crab, and the bag in his arms exploded. It wasn't just a sack; it was a rupture in space. Potions, scrolls, and weapons vomited forth, scattering into the sand.

"Take it! Keep it! Just go!"

Moor watched his companions scramble like seagulls on a chip. Opal snatched a tattoo needle that hummed with eldritch power. Lavender shoved bracers onto her wrists. Moor knelt, his hand closing around a heavy amulet half-buried in the sand. He felt the pulse of it immediately—a connection to the divine, a reservoir of faith. He clipped it over his white robes.

"The mirrors," Moor said, pointing his mace toward the temple entrance. "We can't walk through that light."

+The Burning Path

The heat near the temple entrance was distinct from the humidity. It was dry, sharp, and tasted of ozone. Two massive apes—Girallons, four-armed and foaming—were pacing near the entryway, testing the light beams. Moor watched as one ape brushed a beam; the fur didn't burn, it vanished. The flesh beneath boiled instantly.

"Move the mirrors," Moor commanded, his voice dropping into the register he used for sermons and battlefield triage.

Opal moved to one of the ground-level reflectors. She grunted, muscles coiling under her grey skin, and shoved the heavy stone housing. The beam swung wild, slicing through the humid air.

"Angle it!" Moor shouted.

She heaved. The beam swept across the stone terrace and caught the lead Girallon in the chest. There was no scream, only the wet sound of fluids turning to steam. The beast fell, cooked from the inside out before it hit the ground. The second one, seeing its packmate liquidated, shrieked and fled into the canopy.

"Effective," Aramil noted, his face pale.

"Deadly," Moor corrected. "Single file. Stay in the shadows of the beams."

They breached the antechamber. It was cool inside, silent as aheld breath. The smell of copper and old blood hit them instantly. The ceiling vanished into darkness thirty feet up, but the floor was the concern. It was a gauntlet of pressure plates, flanked by piles of gold that reached their waists.

"Greed," Lavender whispered, her eyes reflecting the glint of coins. "It's literally paved with greed."

"Don't touch it," Moor warned. He stepped up to the first plate. He pressed his mace down. Click.

A blade, three feet of rusted iron, shot up from the floor with the speed of a striking viper.

"Right," Bob said from somewhere inside his hood. "Traps."

"The mural," Aramil called out. He was standing by the far wall, brushing dust off a faded painting. "Look. A man and a crocodile. Standing together. Crossing swords."

Moor looked at the floor. The plates were paired. "Weight distribution," he realized. "It requires two. Walking in lockstep."

He looked at Lavender. "You and me."

"I weigh as much as one of your legs, Moor," the Tiefling said.

"Use the shield. Pile the gold. Just move when I move."

They stepped onto the first set of plates. Moor felt the mechanism depress, a groan of ancient gears beneath the stone. He held his breath. No blade.

"Step," he said.

They moved. Left foot. Right foot. It was a dance of trust in a room designed for murder. Behind them, Opal and Aramil were solving it their own way, brute-forcing the mechanism with the sheer weight of gathered treasure.

They reached the far door—a portcullis held by counterweights. Above the arch, two rusted greatswords hung crossed, mimicking the mural.

"Grab them," Moor said to Opal. "Pull down."

The Goliath reached up, her height making it easy. Moor grabbed the other. They pulled. The resistance was immense, the friction of centuries fighting them, but the metal groaned and the gate began to rise.

"Go!" Moor roared.

Bob and Aramil darted under the rising spikes. Lavender followed. Moor and Opal released the swords and rolled through just as the iron teeth slammed back into the stone.

+The Serpent's Eye

The next chamber was a furnace.

The heat was oppressive, radiating from a central crystalline prism hanging from the ceiling. Beams of sunlight from the roof were hitting it, scattering into searing lances of energy that cut the room into deadly geometric shapes.

"It’s getting hotter," Aramil said, wiping sweat from his brow. "The mechanism... it's cooking us."

"The statue," Bob pointed.

In the center of the room, bathing in the refracted light, was a massive stone head of a snake. Its mouth was open, and from the stone throat, something wet and alive was uncoiling.

It was immense—a constrictor snake forty feet long, its scales shimmering with an unnatural iridescence. It tasted the air, and its head snapped toward Aramil.

"Brother Snake!" Aramil shouted, raising his hands. "We are not food! The monkeys outside—"

The snake struck. It was a blur of muscle. It slammed into Aramil, wrapping around the half-elf before he could finish his plea. Moor heard the sickening crunch of ribs.

"Aramil!" Moor surged forward, calling upon Tyr. A shield of shimmering force sprang up around Lavender as she unleashed a blast of eldritch energy.

Opal roared, a sound that shook dust from the ceiling. She didn't bother with weapons; she slammed her fist into the snake’s flank. The tattoo she’d found on the beach flared with light, and the punch connected with the force of a battering ram.

The snake hissed, tightening its coil. Aramil’s face was turning purple.

"The mirrors!" Moor shouted, realizing the geometry of the room. "Turn the snake statues! Redirect the light!"

Bob was already moving, darting through the shadows. Lavender sent her familiar—a raven named Tika—to distract the beast. The snake snapped, jaws closing around the bird. The familiar vanished in a puff of sulfur.

"My bird!" Lavender screamed. "You killed my bird!"

She unleashed hell. Blasts of force hammered the serpent. Opal grabbed the snake by its tail and heaved, her muscles tearing under the strain. Moor reached through the chaos, his hand glowing with white light, and slapped his palm against Aramil’s exposed leg. Healing energy flooded the half-elf, knitting bone just enough to keep him alive.

Bob reached the stone head. He shoved. The stone ground against stone. The light beam shifted, hitting the crystal at a new angle.

The room flashed white. The heat spiked, then broke. The beam refracted, splitting into four harmless rays that illuminated the exit.

Opal brought her greataxe down. The snake’s head severed with a wet thud.

Silence rushed back into the room, heavy and hot. Aramil gasped on the floor, clutching his chest.

"We need to move," Moor said, hauling the half-elf up. "The sun is setting. And I can hear them."

From the roof above, the sound of boots on stone. Voices. The Red Fangs.

+The King of Feathers

They burst out of the temple’s suffocating interior into the twilight.

They were in a high-walled courtyard, open to the sky. The sun was a dying ember on the horizon, painting the clouds in bruises of purple and red. The air here was cooler, but the danger was absolute.

In the center of the courtyard, beneath a stone archway, a Tyrannosaurus Rex slumbered.

It was magnificent and terrifying, a mountain of scales and muscle rising and falling with deep, rumbling breaths. And hanging directly above its snout, dangling from the archway like a bauble on a holiday tree, was the Golden Snake.

"Don't. Touch. The dinosaur," Moor whispered.

"I can get it," Bob breathed. The halfling was staring at the artifact, his eyes wide. He pulled a pair of boots from his pack—strange, spring-loaded contraptions he’d looted from the foyer. "I can jump."

"Bob," Moor warned.

"I’m fast. I’m quiet."

"They are on the roof," Aramil hissed, pointing upward. Shadows were moving along the rim of the courtyard. The rival party.

"Go," Moor said. "We'll cover you."

Bob didn't hesitate. He sprinted toward the sleeping titan. The boots engaged, magic flaring, and the halfling launched himself into the air. He soared over the T-Rex’s head, a silent arc against the darkening sky.

He reached the archway. He hung by his legs, inverted, and snatched the Golden Snake.

"Got it!" he whispered.

Then the sky exploded.

A fireball, cast from the rim of the wall, roared down into the courtyard. It wasn't aimed at Bob. It was aimed at the dinosaur.

The explosion was deafening. The T-Rex roared—a sound that vibrated in the marrow of Moor’s bones. The beast thrashed, the stone archway crumbling around it. Bob dropped, clutching the idol, his cloak snapping open to slow his fall.

"Run!" Opal screamed.

The courtyard dissolved into madness. The Red Fangs were dropping from the walls, weapons drawn. The T-Rex, blinded by pain and rage, snapped its jaws at anything that moved. A tail the size of a siege tower swept the area, shattering stone pillars.

"To the door!" Moor shoved Lavender toward the exit. "Don't look back!"

They sprinted. Behind them, the screams of the rival adventurers were cut short by the wet crunch of the T-Rex’s jaws.

+The Gauntlet

The run back was a blur of terror.

They hit the pressure plate room. Gas hissed from the vents—poison, thick and green.

"Hold your breath!" Moor commanded.

He grabbed Lavender’s arm and hurled her across the gap. Opal vaulted over a trap, landing heavy but running. Aramil stumbled, gasping, and Moor grabbed him by the collar of his jacket, dragging him through the poison fog.

"Don't you die on me, professor!"

They reached the foyer. The sun was gone. The mirrors were dark. But the jungle outside was alive. The undead—skeletons, zombies, things that had clawed their way out of the Chultian mud—were swarming the entrance.

"The bubble!" Fizzleblast’s voice was a tinny squeak in the distance. "Get to the bubble!"

A wall of rotting flesh stood between them and the blue shimmer of safety.

"Opal!" Moor shouted. "Clear a path!"

The Goliath roared, her bloodaxe glowing crimson. She became a whirlwind of steel, cleaving through bone and rot. Moor raised his shield, the symbol of Tyr flaring with holy light, turning the undead away, burning them with the radiance of judgment.

They crashed through the line. Bob dove, sliding into the sand. Aramil fell, clutching a smaller artifact he’d swiped from the temple—a single obsidian eye. Opal and Lavender tumbled after them.

Moor was the last. He felt a cold, dead hand grab his ankle. He spun, bringing his mace down in a skull-shattering arc, and threw himself across the threshold.

The noise cut out instantly.

Inside the Fizzle Field, it was quiet. The screams of the dying, the roar of the dinosaur, the hiss of the undead—all of it was muted, distant, like a nightmare observed through thick glass.

Moor rolled onto his back, staring up at the stars appearing over the jungle canopy. His lungs burned. His armor felt like it weighed a thousand pounds.

Fizzleblast stood over them, holding a teddy bear, looking terrifyingly calm.

"Did you get it?" the gnome asked.

Bob sat up, reached into his bag, and pulled out the Golden Snake. It gleamed in the starlight, beautiful and cursed.

"Good," Fizzleblast said. "Niles, bring the canoe. We’re leaving."

Moor closed his eyes, listening to the frantic rhythm of his own heart. They were alive. Richer, perhaps. But as he touched the scales of his holy symbol, he felt a tremor in the earth, a deep vibration that seemed to come from the artifact itself.

They had robbed the past. And the past, Moor knew, rarely forgave such debts.

The sun had set on Chult, but Moor had the sinking suspicion that for them, the long night was just beginning.

THE PORT NYANZARU CHRONICLE

THE PORT NYANZARU CHRONICLE

Volume XLIV, Issue 312 -- SUN EXTINGUISHED OVER CHULT; SCHOLARS BLAME "CATASTROPHIC CE
THE PORT NYANZARU CHRONICLE
"From the Harbour to the Horizon, We Print the Truth"
Volume XLIV, Issue 312♦The 7th of Hammer, Year of the Scarlet Witch, 1494 DR♦Port Nyanzaru, Chult♦Price: 5 Squirtons
FRONT PAGE
SUN EXTINGUISHED OVER CHULT; SCHOLARS BLAME "CATASTROPHIC CELESTIAL EVENT" AS DARKNESS ENTERS THIRD DAY
Temple Expedition in Aldani Basin Believed Connected; Entire Rival Adventuring Company Found Dead in Jungle; Port Authorities Urge Calm
By Kwame Obo, Senior Correspondent

The sun has not risen over Chult in three days.

This newspaper has confirmed, through consultation with the Merchant Prince Wakanga O'tamu's personal astrologers, the Priesthood of Savras, and two independent diviners who have since gone mad, that the celestial body commonly referred to as "the sun" is no longer visible in the sky above the Chultan Peninsula. Nor, by all reports arriving via sending stone from the Sword Coast, above Faerun at large. The stars have likewise vanished. The moon offers no light. The darkness is, in the words of Harbormaster Zindar, "not the darkness of night, but of something else entirely."

Public panic, while officially discouraged, is well underway.

The Chronicle has traced the onset of the darkness to the evening following an unsanctioned temple expedition in the deep jungle southwest of the Aldani Basin. A gnomish wizard of modest reputation and immodest wardrobe — identified by port records as one "Fizzleblast," proprietor of a concern called Charisma Company — hired five mercenaries through channels described by the Mercenary Licensing Board as "irregular at best" and departed upriver three days prior. The party returned the following morning aboard rented canoes (the deposit for which remains unpaid) carrying significant quantities of looted artifacts and one fewer rival adventuring company than had entered the jungle.

"They came paddling back like nothing happened," reported Eku, a Chultan guide who observed the party's return from a neighbouring dock. "The big one — enormous, grey-skinned, built like a siege tower — was laughing about chopping a snake in half while blindfolded. The small one in the hood was counting gold. They were all singed. And the sun was gone. I do not think these facts are unrelated."

Today's Weather

Dark. The temperature has dropped noticeably since the sun's disappearance, though the jungle's ambient heat — generated by volcanic activity beneath the peninsula and the sheer density of rotting vegetation — has prevented a true freeze.

WAKANGA'S WONDROUS WICKS
"In these DARK TIMES, illuminate your home with WAKANGA'S WONDROUS WICKS — enchanted candles that burn for 30 days without replacement! Each candle infused with a minor continual flame cantrip by Merchant Prince Wakanga O'tamu's personal apprentices. Available in Jungle Orchid, Sea Breeze, and New Scent: Existential Dread. 50 squirtons per candle. Bulk discounts for taverns and temples. 'When the sun fails, Wakanga's Wicks prevail!'"
CONTINUED COVERAGE
TEMPLE ARTIFACTS SURFACE IN PORT NYANZARU MARKETS; PROVENANCE QUESTIONED
By Adaeze Nri

Within hours of the expedition party's return, several items of considerable magical power began appearing in Port Nyanzaru's open markets. A staff carved from an unfamiliar white wood, radiating restorative energy. A belt of extraordinary craftsmanship that one merchant claimed "made a halfling lift a barrel over his head." A pair of bracers that bent light around themselves. And, most alarmingly, a cloak fashioned from what appears to be living bat membrane, which was observed allowing its wearer to fly short distances above the harbour district.

The Merchant Princes' Trade Commission has issued a formal inquiry into the provenance of these items, noting that the removal of artifacts from Chultan temple sites without governmental license is a violation of the Antiquities Preservation Act of 1489 DR. The gnomish expedition leader, Fizzleblast, holds no such license. His company, "Charisma Company," is not registered with the Port Nyanzaru Bureau of Commerce.

UNDEAD ACTIVITY SURGES ACROSS PENINSULA FOLLOWING CELESTIAL EVENT; FORT BELUARIAN REPORTS "UNPRECEDENTED" ATTACKS
By Kwame Obo

The Order of the Gauntlet garrison at Fort Beluarian has reported a threefold increase in undead encounters since the sun's disappearance. Commander Niles Breakbone — no relation to the gnomish wizard's intern — has dispatched urgent requests to Baldur's Gate for reinforcement, stating that the fort's current complement is "wholly insufficient to manage a peninsula of undead that no longer have a sunrise to drive them back underground."

Reports from jungle guides and merchant caravans paint a grim picture. Creatures that previously emerged only at night now roam freely at all hours. The Death Curse, thought to have been broken two years prior, has resurfaced in whispered rumour, though no confirmed cases of soul-trapping have been reported.

PORT NYANZARU LAMP OIL PRICES SURGE 800%; MERCHANT PRINCE IFAN DENIES PROFITEERING
By Adaeze Nri

The price of lamp oil in Port Nyanzaru has increased eightfold since the onset of the eternal darkness. Merchant Prince Ifan Tal'roa, who controls the city's oil trade, has attributed the increase to "market forces" and "unprecedented demand" rather than personal enrichment. Public sentiment, however, has been less generous.

"He raised the price before the second bell had finished ringing," said a dockworker who requested anonymity. "The sun had been gone for four hours. Four hours! And suddenly a flask of oil costs more than a week's wages. If that is not profiteering, I do not know the meaning of the word."

♦
OPINION & EDITORIAL
"Five Strangers Walk Into Our Jungle, and the Sun Walks Out"
Opinion of Grandfather Zitembe, Priest of Savras, Temple of Divination, Port Nyanzaru

I have served at the Temple of Savras for forty-seven years. I have read the stars. I have interpreted the dreams of princes and paupers alike. I have, on six occasions, correctly predicted the winner of the annual tyrannosaur sprint. My record of prognostication is, I say without false modesty, unimpeachable.

I did not see this coming. None of us did. And that, more than the darkness itself, is what terrifies me.

CLASSIFIEDS & NOTICES
FOR QUESTIONING BY ORDER OF THE COUNCIL OF MERCHANT PRINCES DESCRIPTION: A half-elf of middling height and gaunt build. Wears a battered leather hat and a stained overcoat. Carries a purple crystal on a chain around his neck. Speaks with the cadence of a man who has been denied tenure. Seen clutching a glowing golden object during the expedition's departure from the Aldani Basin. Described by one witness as "the grungy one who talked to the monkeys." ALSO SOUGHT: A gnome of advanced age in an oversized cashmere robe, travelling in a shimmering bubble with a traumatized adolescent assistant. Proprietor of "Charisma Company." Owes 50 gold in canoe rental fees. REWARD: 500 gold for information leading to the recovery of the GOLDEN SERPENT EYE and/or the restoration of the sun. 5 gold for information leading to the collection of the canoe rental debt. Report to: Trade Commissioner Jessamine, Market Ward, or any officer of the Ytepka Society.
♦
HELP WANTED
LAMPLIGHTERS (URGENT -- CITY-WIDE)
"The Port Nyanzaru Harbourmaster's Office seeks FORTY (40) additional lamplighters for immediate deployment. Permanent darkness has rendered existing staff insufficient. Must be comfortable working at heights, in total darkness, and near areas of increased undead activity. Pay: 8 squirtons per day plus hazard supplement. Oil provided. Apply at the Harbourmaster's Office, Tiryki Anchorage. Night shifts only. All shifts are night shifts."
JUNGLE RECOVERY CREW
"EXPERIENCED GUIDES AND PORTERS needed to recover remains and equipment from the Aldani Basin jungle trail. Multiple casualties reported. Must be willing to work in undead-active territory without sunlight. Armed escort provided by the Order of the Gauntlet. 15 squirtons per day. No questions about what happened to the previous recovery crew. Apply at Fort Beluarian."
TEMPLE ASSESSMENT SCHOLARS
"The Council of Merchant Princes seeks QUALIFIED ARCHAEOLOGISTS AND DIVINE SCHOLARS to assess a recently disturbed temple site in the deep jungle. Applicants must hold proper antiquities credentials — adjunct professor status is NOT sufficient. Knowledge of serpent iconography, solar binding rituals, and Chultan religious history required. Danger pay negotiable. Contact Trade Commissioner Jessamine."
CANOE REPAIR SPECIALIST
"SHAGO'S CANOE RENTALS seeks a skilled woodworker to repair fire and acid damage to three (3) river canoes. Damage described as 'consistent with a gnome who does not respect other people's property.' Materials provided. 6 squirtons. Apply dockside, Tiryki Anchorage. Deposit still owed."
♦
LOST & FOUND
LOST: One Golden Serpent Eye
"LOST (or STOLEN): A gemstone of considerable size and warmth, believed to have been pried from a golden serpent idol located in a sealed temple in the Aldani Basin. The gem is described as 'the size of a quail's egg, burning gold in colour, and warm to the touch.' IT IS BELIEVED THAT THE RETURN OF THIS ITEM WILL RESTORE THE SUN. Reward: The Merchant Princes will pay ANY PRICE for its safe return. The Temple of Savras will grant a lifetime of favourable divinations. Contact Grandfather Zitembe at the Temple of Savras or any Merchant Prince's representative. THIS IS NOT A DRILL."
FOUND: One Stained Fedora
"FOUND on the Tiryki Anchorage docks: A wide-brimmed leather hat of a style popular among academic adventurers. Heavily stained with sweat, jungle rot, and what appears to be constrictor venom. Smells of regret. No one has claimed it. If uncollected within seven days, it will be burned. Inquire at SHAGO'S CANOE RENTALS."
MISSING: Members of Blackwater Adventuring Company
"MISSING: The adventuring company known as 'Blackwater' (4-6 members, variously armed, believed to include at least two practitioners of the arcane arts) departed Port Nyanzaru three days ago for a temple expedition in the Aldani Basin. They have not returned. Partial remains were recovered along the jungle trail. Any information regarding surviving members is welcome. Contact the Mercenary Licensing Board, Market Ward."
♦
SHOPS & SERVICES
WAKANGA'S WONDROUS WICKS
"In these DARK TIMES, illuminate your home with WAKANGA'S WONDROUS WICKS — enchanted candles that burn for 30 days without replacement! Each candle infused with a minor continual flame cantrip by Merchant Prince Wakanga O'tamu's personal apprentices. Available in Jungle Orchid, Sea Breeze, and New Scent: Existential Dread. 50 squirtons per candle. Bulk discounts for taverns and temples. 'When the sun fails, Wakanga's Wicks prevail!'"
THE THUNDERING LIZARD TAVERN
"ETERNAL HAPPY HOUR! Since no one can tell what time it is anymore, The Thundering Lizard is pleased to announce that happy hour pricing is now in effect INDEFINITELY. Half-price tej and Chultan rum until the sun returns or the casks run dry, whichever comes first. Live music nightly. Every hour is nightly. Located on Trader's Row, Port Nyanzaru."
EKENE-AFE, LEATHERWORKER
"Has YOUR armour been damaged by giant constrictor snakes, toxic gas, or solar mirror arrays? EKENE-AFE offers expert repairs to all leather and hide armours. Special rates for adventurers returning from temple expeditions. We do not ask questions about provenance. We DO ask that you leave cursed items outside. Shop located near the Grand Coliseum."
♦
Today's Weather

Dark. The temperature has dropped noticeably since the sun's disappearance, though the jungle's ambient heat — generated by volcanic activity beneath the peninsula and the sheer density of rotting vegetation — has prevented a true freeze. Harbour winds blow from the southwest, carrying the smell of brine and, increasingly, of things best left unidentified. The old fishermen have stopped predicting weather entirely, as their methods rely on observing cloud formations, and there are no longer any clouds visible against the featureless black sky. Rain is expected, though how anyone will distinguish it from the general atmosphere of misery is unclear.

Almanac
  • Moon phase: Unknown. The moon has not been visible since the celestial event. Temple astronomers theorize it is still present but "unlit," which raises uncomfortable questions about the nature of moonlight that most citizens would prefer not to contemplate.
  • Tide: Harbour tides continue on their normal schedule, suggesting that whatever force governs the ocean's movements is distinct from whatever force previously governed the sun. Fishermen report the tides are "angrier than usual," though this may be projection.
