  • Holy observance: The Temple of Savras has declared an indefinite Period of Supplication. The Temple of Sune has suspended all services, citing that "beauty requires light to be appreciated." The shrine to Ubtao in the old quarter has seen a surge in attendance from citizens who had not visited in decades.
  • Harvest note: All agricultural activity on the peninsula has ceased. Crops require sunlight. The Merchant Princes have begun emergency grain importation from the Sword Coast, assuming the Sword Coast is similarly motivated to trade in the dark.
♦
OBITUARIES
The Blackwater Company (Estimated 4-6 Members, Names Unconfirmed)

The adventuring company known as Blackwater passed from this world on the jungle trail between the Aldani Basin temple site and the River Soshenstar, during the first hours of the celestial darkness. The company, licensed through the Mercenary Board and reportedly well-equipped, had entered the jungle in pursuit of the same temple artifacts sought by the Charisma Company expedition. They did not reach the temple.

Witnesses — limited to the members of the rival expedition, who have since departed Port Nyanzaru — report that the Blackwater company engaged the Charisma Company party near the temple exit, launching arcane attacks including at least one fireball that struck a sleeping tyrannosaur. The resulting chaos, combined with the sudden onset of total darkness, left Blackwater exposed in hostile jungle without light, shelter, or, it would appear, sufficient survival instincts.

♦
THE FUNNY PAGES
"The New Normal"
Panel 1
A Port Nyanzaru merchant stands behind his stall at midday, surrounded by complete darkness. A sign reads: "FRESH FRUIT — PICKED THIS MORNING." He squints into the void. Caption: "Day three without the sun."
Panel 2
A customer approaches, holding a lantern. "What time is it?" the customer asks. The merchant checks a sundial on his counter. The sundial is blank. "Could be noon," the merchant says. "Could be midnight. I've been here for either four hours or sixteen."
Panel 3
A town crier runs past, ringing a bell. "HEAR YE! THE MERCHANT PRINCES ASSURE YOU THE SUN WILL RETURN SHORTLY! REMAIN CALM!" The merchant and customer watch him go. A zombie shuffles past in the background, unnoticed.
Panel 4
The merchant looks down at his fruit stall. The fruit is wilting. A bat has landed on the mangoes. The customer is gone. The zombie is now browsing the papayas. Caption: "He bought a lamp and raised his prices."

Published by Obo & Nri at the Chronicle House, Merchant Ward, Port Nyanzaru. Printed at the Goldenthrone Pressworks on Pharaoh's Way by light of seventeen enchanted candles (Wakanga's Wondrous Wicks -- sponsor). Subscription: 2 gold per season, delivered by street-runner to your residence or place of business. The Port Nyanzaru Chronicle is not responsible for the accuracy of advertisements, the restoration of celestial bodies, or the emotional wellbeing of readers who previously took the sun for granted. All opinions expressed in the Editorial section are those of the authors and do not necessarily reflect the views of the Merchant Princes, the temples, or the sun, which is no longer available for comment.

Battle Buddies
Session Conversation Analysis

The Fizzleblast one-shot produced a broadly distributed conversation network with no single dominant axis. Moor (Kyle 1) emerged as the primary player-side hub, initiating more plans and DM exchanges than anyone else, while Opal (Shannon) ran a close second with high engagement across all partners.

Lavender (Kelsey) served as the critical bridge between the front-line fighters and the stealth-oriented back row, maintaining meaningful interaction counts with every party member. The DM distributed attention with impressive evenness (8.7/10), though Moor and Opal drew slightly more focus due to their frequent trap-testing and combat actions.

The weakest link in the network was Aramil and Bob, who operated independently for most of the session but converged during the high-stakes finale. Overall, the party's conversation flow reflects a healthy, collaborative group with a strong core triangle of Moor, Lavender, and Opal driving most of the session's momentum.

score
Best Moment
DM Fairness

DM Fairness

Attention distribution across 5 players -- 5 fairness axes -- standard session
Attention Distribution Analysis
This measures how evenly the DM distributed speaking time and engagement across players -- not whether the DM's decisions were fair (see DM Report Card in Moments tab).
Raw Distribution Score: 9.2/10 (Grade A+)
5 fairness dimensions
The DM delivered a masterclass in attention distribution, ensuring every player had at least one unforgettable spotlight moment while maintaining excellent balance across combat, mechanics, and roleplay. The slight natural variation in speaking time reflects organic player personality differences, not DM neglect.

Click any player card below to see their detailed breakdown.

Why this score is expected:
  • This was a one-shot with no established character backstories, making it naturally harder to create equal plot relevance hooks for all five players.
  • Aramil's high scores were largely organic: his archaeologist class and speak-with-animals ability were naturally suited to a temple dungeon, not because the DM deliberately favored him.
  • Bob's player appeared to adopt a more observational, quip-heavy play style in the early session, which naturally reduces DM-directed dialogue. His engagement increased dramatically when given the stealth-focused heist sequence.
  • The real-time pressure of the ticking clock and the one-shot format limited the DM's ability to create individualized RP moments for all five players. The DM did well to give each player at least one signature spotlight moment.
  • Running two characters simultaneously (Kyle 1 as Moor, Kyle 2 as Aramil) for the same physical player created a unique dynamic where DM attention to 'Kyle' was split across two characters.

A CHARISMA COMPANY ONE-SHOT
Charisma Company: Fizzleblast’s Fieldtrip • DM'd by Blue Orbit
